B-3500 Hasselt

CONFIDENTIAL

Style Guide

This document is a template that can be used to create a service catalogue for a customer. This customer may be a new customer or an existing customer undergoing contract renewal. The template includes all services in the English language. To create a service catalogue from this template, proceed as follows:

  1. Save this document as a new document and name it according the naming convention in the contract set.
  2. Remove all services from the document that are not applicable.
  3. Verify if all remaining services contain optional components. These optional components may or may not be delivered. If they are not delivered, they can be removed. If they are delivered, they are not considered options but are simply part of the services.
  4. Replace the text <klant> with the name of the customer
  5. …
  6. Remove all comments from this document.
  7. Accept all changes and stop Track changes.
  8. Renew the contents.
  9. Remove this chapter and save your document.

After these steps you can make changes as necessary and review the service catalogue.

Service description structure

Reading guide Service description

Each Service description in this schedule contains the same paragraphs presenting a uniform template. In this chapter each standard paragraph is explained.

Standard services

This section describes the Services that are to be delivered to <klant>. The Services include any services, activities and tasks that are of a nature and type that would ordinarily be performed as an inherent subtask by an entity (internal or external) performing Services similar to the Services and/or that are generally considered to be part of the Services.

Optional services

This section describes the Services that can be optionally added to the Service delivered to <klant>.

Out of scope

This section describes the explicitly defined out of scope services related to the Service.

Conditions

This section describes the conditions that must be met in order for Cegeka to provide the Service.

Responsibilities

The RACI of a Service is included in this document. The following definitions are used:

  • R = Responsible: Party responsible for performing the activity and/or achieve the task. One party is Responsible, although other Parties can be delegated to assist in the actual work if required. An Accountable must sign off (approve) on work that Responsible provides. Responsible is by default proactive.
  • A = Accountable: Party ultimately accountable for the correct and thorough completion of the task and/or activity as performed by Responsible. For each task and/or activity exactly one Accountable must be specified. Accountable has the mandate to accept/dismiss on work provided by Responsible. Accountable is by default reactive.
  • C = Consulted: Party to be consulted (whose opinions are sought in two-way communication) when performing tasks and/or activities. Consulted may also provide requirements on scope, result expectations, etc. Consulted is by default reactive.
  • I = Informed: Party to be informed / kept up-to-date on tasks and/or activities (just one-way communication). Informed often only on completion of the task and/or activity. Informed implies no formal role in realization and therefore by default reactive.

Integrated Cross-functional Services

Governance

Service management

The objective of the service management process is to enable and support effective communication between Cegeka and <klant> regarding the provided services, helping to ensure the alignment between the expectations of <klant> and the service delivery of Cegeka.

Cegeka aims to provide optimal support of <klant>’s IT processes, by implementing service management.

A service delivery manager is appointed for <klant>. During the service intake process of <klant>, the service delivery manager customizes the Cegeka generic IT service delivery processes and documents this in the service delivery plan. The processes are based on ITIL. The service delivery plan is reviewed at least annually and is updated whenever changes are made to the service. This service delivery plan will be created during the setup service organization stream of the transition. Cegeka manages and guards the agreed service levels.

The service delivery manager consolidates the metrics, defined in the SLA, in the service status report and reports if the agreed-upon service level targets were met or not. The service status report is used to discuss the results with <klant> during the service review meeting.

To meet the objectives of the Service Management process, the Service Delivery Manager operates on all 3 layers within the governance model.

On an operational level this includes the following activities and responsibilities:

  • Coordinate larger (multi-team) changes and smaller projects;
  • Coordinate P1 and P2 incidents, including communication and (RCA) reporting;
  • Perform the Incident Manager role;
  • Perform the Problem Manager role;
  • Functional management of the various Cegeka teams and engineers (daily operational meetings)
  • Have operational meetings with various suppliers and 3rd parties

On a tactical level this includes the following activities and responsibilities:

  • Create, prepare and discuss the monthly performance review;
  • Steer follow-up actions from the performance review;
  • Check and approve timewriting of engineers as part of the invoice preparation;
  • Create and discuss the monthly invoice;

On a strategic level this includes the following activities and responsibilities

  • Steer continuous improvement;
  • Identify and realize cost savings
  • Identify and discuss new services

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager

Architecture management

Lead Architect

The Cegeka Lead Architect is responsible for the architectural design, control and optimization of the contractual services.

This is reflected in his accountability for the availability and quality of the technical plans, e.g. monitoring, availability, back-up and disaster recovery plans. He/she will furthermore support the Service Delivery Manager and Account Manager in creating improvement proposals from a technological perspective based upon the business needs of the customer. He will support the Pre-Sales and Account Manager in designing the solution and calculating the cost when proposing new services to Customer. The Lead Architect:

  • Is the owner and point of contact regarding infrastructure architecture;
  • Develops and maintains the infrastructure roadmap;
  • Participates in all relevant governance bodies of the customer;
  • Is responsible for the development of infrastructure policies, standards and guidelines that direct the selection, development, implementation and use of infrastructure within the enterprise;
  • Communicates all infrastructure architecture decisions, plans, goals, strategies and risks;
  • Functionally describes the Infrastructure Architecture managed by Cegeka of customer in the solution architecture document according to customer standards;
  • Focuses continuously on identifying opportunities to optimize and improve the infrastructure of customer to increase performance and/or reduce costs;
  • Defines the necessary business cases & translate business needs from the Customer into high-performance and coherent ICT solutions;
  • Delivers high level capacity & performance forecast coming 18 months;
  • Is accountable for the Customer Landscape Documentation (HLD, SPOF’s, …).

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0040_0010_M_TM.000 Lead Architect

The Cegeka Technical Architect is responsible for the technical architecture, operations and optimization of the contractual services. This is reflected in e.g. preparing a monitoring, availability, back-up and disaster recovery plan. Configuration Management is also operated by the Technical Architect. He/she will furthermore support the Service Delivery Manager and Account Manager in creating proposals from a technological perspective. He will support the Pre-Sales and Account Manager in calculating the cost when proposing new services to Customer but will also provide cost estimates and impact analyses for RFC’s. The Technical Architect:

  • Is responsible for the IT infrastructure landscape drawings;
  • Determines the Infrastructure or application to meet the Service Levels of Customer;
  • Is responsible for the correctness and completeness of the CMDB and license compliancy;
  • Is responsible for lifecycle management and signalling related risks;
  • Is responsible for vulnerability compliance and signalling related risks;
  • Forecasting capacity needs for coming 18 months;
  • Supports planned migrations and upgrades;
  • Design the various technical plans (monitoring, backup, patching, disaster recovery & security plans);
  • Proposes optimisations for running solutions at Customer, in close cooperation with the Service Delivery Manager;
  • Validates the technical feasibility, from an infrastructure architecture perspective, of projects and major changes;
  • Creates technical designs for Projects & Complex RFC’s;
  • Collaborates with engineers for the impact analyses of Changes (Complex RFC’s) and assists in proposal phases of the RFC’s (costs);
  • Supports system engineers in questions and multi-disciplinary Changes.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0040_0020_M_TM.000 Technical Architect

Incident management

The primary goal of the incident management process is to restore normal service operation as quickly as possible when incidents occur and minimize the adverse impact on <klant>’s IT and/or business operations, thus ensuring that the agreed levels of service quality and availability are maintained.

'Normal service operation' is defined here as service operation as defined in the Service Levels. Cegeka’s Incident Management responsibilities span the services delivered by Cegeka and include:

  • Create (security) incident records in the service management tool according to pre-agreed (security) incident classifications between Cegeka and <klant>. Incident classifications will be defined in the Service Delivery Plan (SDP);
  • Categorizing, documenting and communicating to <klant> the relative importance of each incident according to the severity levels described in the Incident management classification matrix;
  • Tracking and managing all incidents in the <klant> infrastructure assigned to Cegeka;
  • Maintaining regular communications (as agreed in the operational documentation) of the status of an incident between all parties and <klant> Authorized Users until the incident is resolved;
  • Providing prompt notification to <klant> of Service outages related to managed systems
  • Providing <klant> with regular and timely progress updates that clearly indicate the nature of the applicable incident, the estimated time to completion, and potential short-term alternatives agreed to by <klant> and Cegeka;
  • Resolving those incidents arising from or related to the Services;
  • Meeting specified Service Levels;
  • Providing reports to <klant> regarding incidents as relevant;
  • In case of a major incident: provide <klant> with a service improvement report;
  • Communicating the status of Severity Level 1 incidents (and ad-hoc for Severity Level 2 incidents) on a timely basis appropriate to the impact of the incident to <klant> and agreed to by <klant> and Cegeka in accordance with the operational documentation;
  • If Cegeka believes an incident cannot be resolved, Cegeka will communicate to <klant> the nature of the incident, the reason why Cegeka believes it cannot be Resolved and the efforts employed by Cegeka to try to resolve the incident to the appropriate level within <klant> as agreed to by <klant> and Cegeka;
  • Performing proactive, where possible and based on monitoring, and reactive troubleshooting to identify and resolve incidents;
  • Employing procedures for proactive monitoring, logging, tracking, escalation, review, and reporting (historical and predictive) for appropriate incidents in accordance with the operational documentation;
  • Correlation for all incidents identifying and document preventive measures and making recommendations to fix root causes. Perform root cause analysis as part of problem management;
  • Regularly updating the incident management tracking system (including the knowledge database) in accordance with agreed to procedures by both Parties and in accordance with Cegeka’s good Industry practice as they are developed.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0040_M_TM.000 Incident Manager

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager (Act in smaller teams also as Incident Manager)

Request management

Cegeka is responsible for tracking, managing, processing and fulfilling <klant> appropriately approved requests in accordance with the operational documentation. These requests are standard and non-standard requests for provided new, contracted Services and/or managed infrastructure.

A request will always be raised with priority P4.

Cegeka’s request management responsibilities include:

  • Tracking, managing, processing and fulfilling requests in accordance with the operational documentation;
  • Entering all requests into the Cegeka request tracking system;
  • Performing an analysis of the request requirements. Cegeka personnel will, if required, contact <klant>;
  • Requestor to ensure proper understanding/clarification of the request;
  • Providing the requested information;
  • Fulfilling the approved request.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0020_M_TM.000 Change Manager

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager (Acts in smaller teams also as Change Manager)

Problem management

The problem Management process is responsible for managing the lifecycle of all problems related to the Services. The primary objectives of Problem Management are to avoid incidents from happening, to eliminate recurring incidents and to minimize the impact of those incidents that cannot be prevented.

Cegeka Problem Management responsibilities include:

  • Resolving Problems related to the Services according to defined Service levels;
  • Logging and investigation of Problems reported by <klant> authorized users, Cegeka incident managers or in case of recurring incidents and severity 1 and ad-hoc for severity 2 incidents;
  • Maintaining clear accountability within Cegeka and to <klant> for problem management and resolution;
  • Monitoring, controlling and managing each problem arising from or relating to the Services until it is resolved, and confirmed with the authorized user in accordance with the operational documentation;
  • Maintaining regular communication (as agreed in the operational documentation) of the status of problems between all parties and authorized users until the problem is resolved;
  • Performing for each logged P1 problem a root cause analysis, identifying and documenting preventive measures and making recommendations to fix root causes. Root cause analysis reports (to be presented to <klant>) will include a description of the failure, root cause analysis performed, proposed actions (with timing and the Party responsible to implement such actions) to eliminate or reduce the likelihood of a recurrence of the failure, and confirmation that proposed actions addressed the Problem. Execution of problem fixing will be handled through Change Management policies;
  • Implementing corrective measures or workaround actions to avoid unnecessary reoccurrence of incidents and problems.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0030_M_TM.000 Problem Manager

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager (Act in smaller teams also as Problem Manager)

Change management

Change Management is the process responsible for managing lifecycle of all Changes. The primary objective of Change Management is to standardize the process of introducing and executing changes to the managed infrastructure. These changes may arise reactively in response to problems or externally imposed requirements (e.g. legislative changes), or proactively from seeking improved efficiency and effectiveness or to enable or reflect business, programs, projects or service improvement initiatives.

Cegeka will use its Change Management Tracking system to track changes relating to the scope of this contract. Standard Changes are defined as standard, non-complex changes in the datacenter or managed infrastructure in scope, with pre-defined impact assessments and price, executed according to pre-defined procedures. Moreover, Standard Changes must have the following attributes:

  • The tasks relate directly to one or several configuration items already managed under the contract by Cegeka or : the execution of the “install” task(s) will provide <klant> with similar configuration items (e.g. creation of a virtual Linux server with storage, or creation of a hypervisor system);
  • No scoping or project management by Cegeka is required for the execution of the tasks since the execution is related to standardized configuration items known to <klant> and Cegeka;
  • Pre-approved and not require handling by the CAB.

Changes that are handled through the Change Management process are characterized by any of following:

  • The tasks to carry out are defined in the Standard Change catalogue as an activity; or
  • The change consists of several different Standard Changes to carry out simultaneously or consecutively for which coordination by Cegeka is required in order to realize successfully;
  • The tasks to carry out need further technical investigation in order to prepare the planning and execution successfully.

The tasks to carry out are complex because of interdependencies and need close co-operation with <klant> in order to be implemented (e.g. in case of Managed infrastructure adaptations in an application release weekend).

The change management process does not handle <klant>’s requests (a.k.a. nonstandard service requests) for new Services/Products/Solutions that have been categorized as Cegeka Projects by <klant>. These will follow the project management agreement.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0020_M_TM.000 Change Manager

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager (Act in smaller teams also as Change Manager)

Configuration management

The configuration management process is responsible for creating and maintaining a library of information and documentation for any existing, new, enhanced or modified configuration item of the IT Infrastructure within scope of the Services. Configuration management is a joined effort of Cegeka and <klant>. Each party needs to supply up-to-date, correct and complete information to be able to create and maintain the configuration management system (CMS).

Cegeka will use its configuration management system to track configuration items (CI’s) relating to the scope of this contract.

The following table shows if CI types are in the CMDB of <klant> or at Cegeka. Each company is responsible for keeping its respective CI’s accurate and up-to-date. CI types that are in multiple CMDB’s are managed by both <klant> and Cegeka in their own CMDB tool.

CI Type

<klant>

Cegeka

Software (Business Application)

X

Laptops (end-user devices)

X

Printers

X

Servers (physical and virtual)

X

Network components (switches, routers, AP’s, firewalls, etc.)

X

X

People (contacts)

X

X

<klant> will not have real-time insight in the Cegeka CMDB. A report containing an export of the content of the CMDB can be delivered. This is an automated process.

Cegeka’s configuration management responsibilities include:

  • Maintaining the configuration structures for all of the Configuration Item (Cis) for the Services in scope, which will include at a minimum the ownership, relation to other CI’s, CI unique name, CI location, CI category (e.g. network switch, hardware server, virtual server, application software) CI hardware/software version, IP addresses if applicable, associated middleware and application. The configuration management process by Cegeka will allow for clear identification of software CI’s on hardware CI’s in order to create a comprehensive Infrastructure landscape of <klant>;
  • Ensuring that CI’s may only be added, changed or removed in accordance with the change management policies;
  • Verifying, on a periodic basis, the reliability of the CI data stored. The verification may include the use of auto-discovery tools that will provide automated updates to the stored CI information;
  • Maintaining the CI information in the CMS and providing extracts of the CMS, if required, to <klant> on a regular basis, as agreed in the operational documentation;
  • Reporting on the CI’s and detecting anomalies;
  • The CMS that is used by Cegeka is suitable for integrated Incident, problem and change management processes. Additional information is stored per CI to support the DR/BC process.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0040_0020_M_TM.000 Technical Architect

Availability management

The goal of Availability Management is to optimize availability of the managed infrastructure including the IT Infrastructure, services and supporting organization, to deliver a cost effective and sustained level of availability that enables the business to satisfy its business objectives.

Cegeka’s Availability Management process is embedded via the same monitoring process as the one for Capacity Management.

Cegeka ensures to have ample resources available for the future by using monitoring tools to detect trends (what we know) and capture required growth from <klant> orders and quotations and our own growth predictions, etc. (what we expect). This service applies to all the Shared Platform functionality, including storage performance and capacity, virtual computing, networking and also environmental like power, cooling, protective measures, data room space, etc.

The lead architect is responsible for defining the monitoring plan. Infrastructure availability reports will be attached to the service review report.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Service Manager

Capacity management

Capacity management is part of Cegeka’s continuous service delivery and shall ensure that IT resources are sized right to meet current and future business requirements in a cost-effective manner. It covers multiple aspects from capacity analysis to capacity planning.

Capacity analysis is concerned with:

  • Monitoring the performance and throughput or load on a (virtual) server, server farm, storage, network or other infrastructure component;
  • Performance analysis of measurement data, including analysis of the impact of new projects or releases on capacity;
  • Performance tuning of activities to ensure the most efficient use of existing infrastructure;
  • Capacity planning is understanding the demands on the Services and future plans for workload growth or shrinkage, including planning and acting upon.

Component capacity management

Component capacity management is embedded in Cegeka’s monitoring process. The Cegeka monitoring process provides various options for automated monitoring and alerting. If there are spikes in, for example, processing power at a particular time of the day, it shall analyse what is happening at that time and make Changes to maximize the existing IT infrastructure, for example, move a batch cycle to a quieter period.

It is implemented as follows: processing which deviates above installed thresholds, will generate an alert, which is handled via the incident management process. Recurring alerts will be handled through the problem management process for the alert analysis and for the solution, which is implemented via the Change management. This component capacity management is imbedded in the daily operational processes for <klant>.

The monitoring team implements the monitoring and alerting scheme. Alerts generate an incident, which is handled through the Incident Management process. Monitoring data is accessible and available for review (performance management). The monitoring data is retained for at least one year.

During service delivery, the technical architect keeps the monitoring plan up-to-date and the service delivery manager reviews the monitoring plan at least once a year. Specific thresholds, work instructions and process interfaces will be agreed and elaborated on during Transition.

Resource consumption is measured on infrastructure level and part of the monthly service reporting. Following capacity documents are available:

  • CPU usage;
  • Memory usage;
  • Disk capacity;
  • Database capacity.

Capacity trending

Another aspect of capacity management is capacity trending. As the usage of the IT infrastructure and Services Change and functionality evolves, resource usage can Change and lead to capacity shortage if not managed. Capacity trending is based on historical data, enriched with impact from known Change (known to Cegeka) and extrapolated to the near future.

For <klant>, capacity trending shall be analysed and formally reported by Cegeka.

SCOPE

Non-exhaustive list:

  • Linux
  • Windows
  • Oracle databases
  • SQL databases
  • Network (internet, LAN, Firewall, load balancing)
  • Storage and Backup
  • Citrix and VDI
  • Tape Library

FREQUENCY

As agreed in the service delivery plan.

MEETING

Reported in Architecture Review Meeting

CONTENT

The report contains:

  • Management summary;
  • Analysis & findings;
  • Graphs or tables based on capacity data;
  • Cegeka’s advice for up- or downscaling, for adding or reducing capacity, based on upcoming Changes in the system architecture.

The capacity reports will be used by Cegeka for pro-active maintenance purposes. The pro-active maintenance process is part of the problem management process. The Cegeka technical architect will analyse the capacity management reports in combination with monitoring trend reports.

Detailed problem analysis will be performed by the Cegeka engineering team and with following deliverables as output:

  • Proposal for capacity adjustments via change management.
  • Monitoring improvements.
  • Proposal for improvements (upgrades).

Capacity planning

The demand governance of the architecture board will capture, analyse and consolidate upcoming Changes, and will trigger high-level capacity plans in an early stage. This, on top of the capacity trending in place, will give a proactive, upfront, consolidated plan for adding capacity in anticipation of an increase in demand.

Service Part number(s)

0.0010_0040_0010_M_TM.000 Lead Architect

0.0010_0040_0020_M_TM.000 Technical Architect

Responsibilities

Continuity management

Cegeka’s Lead Architect is at the centre of the process, assisted by the Service Delivery Manager, Account Manager, Security Expert and Technical Architect(s) and gathering input from both <klant> and Cegeka’s architects.

Their activities are based on two building blocks:

  • Continuous improvement and innovation
  • Safeguarding the actual quality of the services

An innovation meeting is organized, and execution and follow-up of this workshop is part of our ISAE 3000 SOC 2 audit.

Continuous improvement and innovation

Continuous innovation always follows the same standard process approach, regardless of the level of focus (technical, process or governance):

Phase 1: Establish baseline – the baseline relies upon the corner stones of operations – architecture, service performance and cost – which are kept up to date as part of the daily operations.

Phase 2: Identify opportunities – opportunities are detected throughout operations. While almost anyone in the service organisation and <klant>’s organisation can identify opportunities, it is the responsibility of our Lead Architect to keep track and prioritise them. During half-yearly workshops prepared by the Lead Architect, the topics are prepared with a clear vision in mind (see “Continuous innovation workshop”).

Phase 3: Develop capture strategy – based on the list of identified and agreed opportunities a strategy is developed to ensure how the opportunities can be “captured” and integrated in the organisation’s planning via an implementation roadmap. Smaller opportunities with a short-term return can already be initiated as an RFC via the existing change management process.

Phase 4: Realise benefits – the roadmap provides the plan for implementation and must contain the business case on which the priority decisions have been made. Phase 4 contains the actual implementation of the opportunity at whatever level it may be – technical, procedure/process level, governance – and looks back at the initial business case(s) to ensure the benefits are realised.

Safeguarding the actual quality of the services the continuous improvement methodology is applied to a number of architecture deliverables, which have interfaces with one another and provide input to the continuous improvement roadmap. An example of the deliverables plotted over time, is given below:

Deliverables that are continuously maintained:

  • Security Plan
  • Backup Plan
  • Disaster Recovery Plan
  • Monitoring Plan
  • Patch Plan
  • Innovation meeting
  • Enterprise Architecture Document
  • Solution Architecture Document
  • Technology Architecture Principles
  • Lifecycle Management Plan

The implementation of these deliverables and the yearly update is part of our ISAE 3000 SOC2 audit.

Innovation meeting

The continuous innovation workshops are key in our process. About one month before the actual workshop, Cegeka will start preparing it with a core team of Service Delivery Manager, Account Manager and Technical Architects coordinated by Cegeka’s Lead Architect. To ensure consistency and synergy Cegeka’s process owner of continuous innovation as well as the Lead Architects’ manager are involved.

This core team puts together a draft agenda using a subset of <klant>’s business drivers acting as central theme and a high-level roadmap showing both ongoing initiatives and new opportunities. The existing and continuously managed architecture overview is referenced wherever needed. The draft agenda is also discussed with <klant> prior to the meeting to ensure expectations are aligned.

For each continuous innovation topic, a proposal is prepared containing:

  • The current situation;
  • The proposal itself;
  • The pros and cons of the proposal;
  • The financial aspects: implementation cost, recurring cost & impact on TCO and monthly invoice;
  • KPI’s allowing to demonstrate the actual realisation of the expected benefits.

These proposals are presented and discussed during the workshop itself to reach a consensus on how to proceed (or not) with the proposed initiatives. The outcome can range from approval over further discussion or in-depth workshops to postponement or cancellation.

As part of the workshop, Cegeka’s solution portfolio and its implementation roadmap is presented in support of the proposed opportunities and to share our vision on the near future.

Service desk

Introduction

The primary focus of the service desk is to provide customer end-user with a single point of contact for incidents, general questions, information requests, change requests or complaints concerning the infrastructure or applications of customer.

Cegeka will set up a service desk that shall be responsible in providing the following services related to customer in accordance with the agreed Service Levels:

  • A Single Point of Contact for customer end-user for incidents, general questions, information requests, change requests or complaints concerning the infrastructure or applications of customer;
  • Cegeka provides a skilled service desk that is not just responsible for call catch & dispatch but also for resolving tickets and for contributing to the knowledge database;

Cegeka will also ensure that the quality is guaranteed by clear processes, swift communication flows, well determined ownership, performance indicators related to availability, timeliness and first time right to customer.

Service description

Standard services

  • The Cegeka service desk shall act as Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for customer and shall be responsible for:
  • Receiving, registering, prioritizing, categorizing, logging and closing of calls/tickets in Cegeka ITSM tool;
  • Giving the user a ticket number and an immediate solution (this can be a work around) or relevant answer if possible;
  • Identification of the cause of an incident as far as possible;
  • Dispatching, assignation, escalation of requests or incidents (or other call types) to the appropriate resolver group;
  • Informing users about the status of a ticket;
  • Registration of solutions for incidents;
  • Outside business hours, agreed customer authorized roles can contact the Cegeka service desk to report Priority 1 incidents (by phone mandatory);
  • Tickets are assigned to a specific resolver group by Cegeka service desk. These resolver groups can be Cegeka, customer or third party resolver groups;
  • Cegeka will provide the authorized roles with the instructions and manuals concerning the processes, the tools and the environment itself to obtain a good understanding of the complete chain of incident/request/standard change management;
  • Cegeka service desk uses a knowledge management tool, to document first line procedures, work instructions, knowledge, workarounds, contact persons, and is essential to improve service desk resolution rate (and the speed to resolve an incident). It is also used to train service desk agents. The content is continuously updated based on daily experiences and follows an approval process before Changes are

published to all service desk agents. The Incident Manager is accountable for the information in the Cegeka knowledge management system;

Optional services

  • VIP support – supporting a limited amount of named customer VIPS with priority and due care.
  • Third party liaison function for tickets raised at
  • SPOC-function for tickets initiated by third parties, including incidents, requests and problem tickets

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

  • The Customer will provide a list of authorized users, who will be allowed to contact the service desk during a specific time window.
  • The Customer will provide Cegeka with a list of authorized partners and authorized requests per service window.
  • Requests (standard changes, non-standard changes, information requests…) will only be registered if they are filed through the appropriate channel by an authorized user of a service.

Part number

0.0020_0010_0010_M_S.000 Service Desk - VIP Ticket

0.0020_0010_0020_A_S.000 Service Desk - Standard Ticket Dutch

0.0020_0010_0030_A_S.000 Service Desk - Standard Ticket English

Self Service function

Introduction

The self-service function delivers an integrated request and order portal platform to Customer end users. It can help organizations end users to have direct access to common IT services and components. It provides end-users a function to obtain self-help independently and is constant available to the end-user. It increases the autonomy of the end user and improves the user experience and satisfaction.

It helps organisations to increase end-user productivity, reduce costs, improve resolution times, and meet compliance standards. This is achieved by a high level of automated request fulfilment available through the self service function.

The self-service function is a website (portal) for ordering predefined services and for handling standard changes using workflow automation and authorization flows. The portal is part of the Cegeka service desk ticketing tool. Every request through the service function is also logged as a ticket in the service desk ticketing tool for tracking purposes.

If a request can be handled automatically, the ticket will automatically be resolved. If the request cannot be handled automatically, the ticket will be transferred to the appropriate resolver group.

The self-service portal can be used to:

  • Order IT products and services based on an integrated approval workflow
  • Open, consult, change and close requests
  • Log incidents

Service description

Standard features

The portal consists of a dedicated frontend and a shared backend environment. The backend is composed of an orchestrator (platform and licenses) and all support activities to maintain the solution.

Within the self-service portal there are two types of tickets. Those that are totally automated and those that need a manual intervention. All types of requests will create a ticket in the service desk tool for follow-up by assigned resolver groups.

Automation of requests depend on the integration level with the Customer’s network and Cegeka’s automation layer.

The following standard self-service items are available for Customer:

USER MANAGEMENT

ASSET MANAGEMENT

  • Create New User Account
  • Request new workplace
  • Remove User Account
  • Restage, reinstall desktop/laptop
  • Reset Password by Admin
  • Request additional peripheral
  • Reset my password
  • Replace asset
  • Enable User Account
  • Decommission asset
  • Disable User Account

MAIL MANAGEMENT

APPLICATION MANAGEMENT

  • Enable Mailbox
  • Request application
  • Disable Mailbox
  • Request application removal
  • New Shared Mailbox
  • Buy non-standard software license
  • Remove Shared Mailbox

 

  • Add Member(s) To Shared Mailbox

PERMISSIONS

  • Remove Member(s) From Shared Mailbox
  • Request Remote Access (VPN)
  • Change Member(s) Rights to Shared Mailbox
  • Remove Remote Access (VPN)
  • Increase Mailbox Size
  • Request File Server Folder Access
  • New Mail Distribution Group
  • Remove File Server Folder Access
  • Remove Distribution Group
  • Request File Server Drive Mapping
  • Add Member(s) To Distribution Group
  • Remove File Server Drive Mapping
  • Remove Member(s) From Distribution Group
  • Add User(s) towards AD Security Group
  • New Mail Contact
  • Remove User(s) from AD Security Group
  • Remove Mail Contact
Optional services
  • Customer specific service catalogue items (for e.g. requests for facility, HR items)
  • Service catalogue integration of 3rd party service items and fulfilment
  • Integration of the Cegeka service catalogue items into a central service catalogue portal
  • Development can be offered to custom workflows which automate specific Customer’s requests.

Conditions

The Customer will provide a list of authorized users, who will be allowed to utilize the self-service portal.

The Customer will provide Cegeka with a list of user who are allowed to approve requests and with the desired approval flow.

Part number

  • 0.0030_0010_0010_A_S.000 Self help portal fully automated Ticket (tooling cost)
  • 0.0030_0010_0020_A_S.000 Self help portal manual intervention Ticket
  • 0.0030_0010_0030_M_S.000 Self Service Portal (Base Fee)
  • 0.0030_0010_0040_M_E.000 Self Service portal User licenses

On-site Support

As part of Cegeka’s workplace offering, we offer IMACD and Break & Fix onsite services on a global scale. Below a description of these services can be found.

IMACD

IMACD’ stands for Installs, Moves, Adds, Changes and Disposal – the five primary needs of IT infrastructure.
With more and more companies gearing up to meet the fast-paced challenges of the business world, the need for IMACD services is growing. The Cegeka IMACD services offer long and short-term IMACD solutions for a wide range of organizational needs.

Our services complement our workplace outsourcing services by providing skilled professionals, with extensive knowledge, unique skill sets and the ability to handle peak workloads; thus allowing Customers to concentrate on core business areas. We offer immediate access to our trained staff and the tools to support both your non-routine and unique needs, to ensure business continuity.

We handle IMACD activity of any size or scope and provide IMACD services in and out of normal working hours, to give your business the most flexibility.

The following list provides you a list of IMACD activities:

ACTIVITY

TYPE

DESCRIPTION

DEVICE

Install

Workplace staging

  • Unwrap workplace
  • Attach asset tag + update CMDB
  • Configure hardware
  • Stage workplace – software distribution
  • Repackage for transport
  • Desktop PC
  • Laptop
  • Mobile
  • Tablet

Workplace install

  • Unwrap workplace
  • Install and connection of cabling
  • Discard packaging
  • Desktop PC
  • Laptop
  • Mobile
  • Tablet

Peripheral install

  • Unwrap peripheral
  • Test DOA (Dead on Arrival)
  • Attach asset tag + update CMDB
  • Install and connection of cabling
  • Configuration (printer, scanner)
  • Monitor
  • Docking
  • Printer
  • Scanner

Move

Move workplace or peripheral

  • Contact end user for appointment
  • Disconnect workplace/peripheral
  • Transport to new location
  • Reconnect workplace/peripheral

  • External drives
  • DVD/CD drives
  • Maintain CMDB
  • Peripherals

  • Maintain CMD if applicable

Disposal

  • Disconnect cabling
  • Disposal of hardware
  • Update CMDB
  • Workplaces
  • Peripherals

Break & Fix

The Cegeka Break & Fix services (onsite service) are available in two standard flavours: basic and premium.

Cegeka basic onsite services will establish the necessary service management structures with local staff to be able to act according to agreed SLAs in case of hardware failure. In this case hardware for spare parts is owned by the Customer’s organization and the availability on site is also ensured by the Customer. To assist in this preparation, Cegeka of course is able to plan and organize all necessary tasks together with the Customer to prepare the environment for SLA-based onsite operation (optional).

Cegeka premium onsite services will establish the necessary service management structures with local staff including the necessary spare part handling to be able to act according to agreed SLAs. In this case hardware for spare parts is owned by the Customer’s organization but the local availability as well as care pack repair in case of defects and refill of spare stocks, tracking & reporting is managed by Cegeka as part of the service.

The standard SLA is ‘end of next business day’ and will ensure that incidents are fixed by the end of the next local business day at the latest.

It is possible to agree on specific SLAs, spare policies and local tasks as part of Cegeka’s individual onsite service.

Security Operations Centre

Introduction

The Security Operations and Analytics Services consist of Security Operations Center (SOC) services and a Security Operations and Analytics Platform (SOAP). The SOAP is provided from the European Cegeka datacenters and offer real time security analytics and alerting.

Security Operations Center Services

+ Security Operations and Analytics Platform

= Cegeka Security Operations and Analytics Services

The SOC services provide expert security knowledge, security experience and security tooling to develop, document and execute policies, processes and procedures about security on dedicated platforms, which are deployed inside <klant>’s environment.

Security Operations Center services

The SOC services are delivered by the Cegeka SOC and include the operational day-to-day activities needed to detect and respond to security incidents. The aim of the SOC is to:

  • Detect and respond to threats;
  • Increase resilience by learning about the changing threat landscape;
  • Identify and address security policy breaches or deviations from security best practices;
  • Deliver input for risk management;
  • Visualize security indicators.

Security incident management

Cegeka monitors the alarms generated by the SOAP and uses this platform for “hunting” undiscovered alerts. Next to security incident discovery, the aim of hunting is to detect new opportunities for the production or modification of scenarios.

The output of the SOAP includes system outputs, trend analysis, anomalies and Cegeka threat intelligence. After analysis, the output is declared as a security incident, classified as Priority 1, 2, 3 or 4 and communicated to the appropriate resolution group according to an agreed communication plan. Depending on the priority of the incident, this can be by email or phone or any other communication channel and will be based on (an) existing escalation process(es) as much as possible. Cegeka provides a recommended remediation, and assists the resolution team in charge with performing root cause and impact analysis and managing the status of the security incident ticket. The problem management process is used to investigate the lessons learned and propose additional measures. The change management process is used to implement the additional measures.

Service Part number(s)

0.0040_0020_0040_M_TM.000 Siem governance (monthly)

Security Operations and Analytics Platform

The SOAP gives Cegeka SOC the tool to quickly detect and respond to internal and external attacks. The platform simplifies threat management while minimizing risk and safeguarding <klant> business. The SOAP is built on the Cegeka Operational Intelligence Platform (SOIP). Running from the Cegeka datacenters located in Hasselt (Belgium) and/or Geleen (Netherlands), it provides full end-to-end monitoring of the <klant> private- and public clouds.

The SOAP provides insight into machine data generated from security technologies such as network, endpoint, access, malware, vulnerability and identity information.

Reporting and dashboards

The SOAP provides a library of security posture dashboards. This way Cegeka has a view on security events by location, host, source type, asset groupings and geography. KPIs provide trending and monitoring of your security posture.

The SOAP comes with predefined key indicators that identify key security metrics for the security domains in scope. Scheduled reports deliver key indicators by email.

Cegeka provides <klant> a secured webportal only accessible over the internal <klant> network showing:

  • Trending of notable events;
  • Top risk scores of systems and users;
  • Suspicious connections (inbound, outbound, port scans);
  • Failed access activity (e.g. department, error types);
  • Ongoing and past security incidents (e.g. priority, status, type);
  • Vulnerability scanning and compliance scan results;
  • Published scheduled reports.

Remarks:

  • All information is delivered in form of one or more reports.
  • Depending of the content, reports are refreshed maximum every 24 hours.

Cegeka provides <klant> below standard reports:

  • Number of systems in monitoring (e.g. % vs CMDB);
  • Sysmon report;
  • Active Directory activity report;
  • Security posture report;
  • Audit report related to the integrity of the data in the platform;
  • Access report;
  • Network report.

Remarks:

  • Additional reports can be created on request through the change process.

Service Part number(s)

O.0420_0010_0010_A_S.000 Siem SIEM subscription

O.0420_0010_0020_A_S.000 Siem SIEM data log consumption

O.0420_0010_0030_A_S.000 Siem Back-end systems in scope

O.0420_0010_0040_A_S.000 Siem Workstation systems in scope

A,R

Security Expert

The security expert profile is used to ensure that the delivered Services are in line with the agreed Standards and as defined in the security agreement. As such, the security expert can be used for compliance, audit, privacy, security reporting, awareness and advisory activities on any security and compliance matter during the Term of the Agreement.

The Security expert liaises between the <klant> security & compliance actors and the Cegeka core and technical teams. The security expert can:

  • Serve the Cegeka delivery teams to ensure that the Service implementations are compliant with the <klant> Standard;
  • Serve <klant> custom needs;
    • To ensure that all related risks of the Services in scope are detected, remediated and reported. This includes:
      • data leakage notification;
      • vulnerability notification;
      • security attack notification;
      • data integrity & quality issue notification;
      • security patch & updates notification;
      • security patch resolution.
    • To ensure that security and compliance incidents, problems and changes are handled appropriately;
    • To provide regular updates about the status of the implemented preventive technical measures, threats and security and compliance incidents;
  • Serving both <klant> and Cegeka team as an advisor about specialized security and compliance topics;
  • Maintaining the security and compliance policy and embed this in Cegeka’s organization.

Service Part number(s)

  • 0.0040_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Security Expert

Disaster Recovery testing

Introduction

Cegeka will perform one DR test every year of <klant> DR plan. This will be done in coordination with <klant>: yearly, the date and scope of the DR test will be defined and executed on the planned date or dates. The DR testing must be carried out with minimal impact on the availability of the production environment.

Service description

Standard services

The test will consist of:

  • Preparation:
    • Review the (in-scope) DR test scripts which are permanent available;
    • Meeting(s) with involved Cegeka and <klant> personnel to communicate the scope and to align on activities from both sides. During the meeting, parties will agree on the end state after the DR test (may be ‘back to original state’ or ‘failed over to other side’);
    • Preparation of the activities on both sides;
  • Execution of the DR test:
    • Execute the DR test on the planned date and time according to the DR runbook. Cegeka is in the lead of the DR test and will steer the involved people on activities to be executed;
    • During the test, evaluations will be made to check if RTO and RPO states are achieved as agreed;
    • At the end of the DR test, the environment must be in an agreed end state;
  • Evaluation:
    • Delivery of a disaster recovery test;
    • Evaluation of the execution of the DR test. Where needed, improvement actions will be defined and executed to assure a successful next DR test.

Scenario’s for Cegeka Virtual Infrastructure

For the DR testing of the Cegeka Virtual Infrastructure, there are several Disaster Recovery scenarios. Those scenarios are listed below. In order for a service to survive a disaster, it has to meet certain requirements:

  • The associated servers hosting the service must run on a tier 1 VM;
  • Required connectivity must be offered to both Cegeka’s datacenters;
  • RTO/RPO requirements that can’t be satisfied by using tier 1 VM’s must be fulfilled on the application level. Databases for example often have their own synchronization mechanism to provide an RPO of 0 (zero data loss).

1: Disaster at Cegeka, rendering the primary datacenter unusable:

  • Tier 3 virtual machines will be stopped;
  • Tier 1 virtual machines will be started in the secondary datacenter;
  • WAN connectivity between Cegeka and <klant> will survive, since both datacenters are redundantly connected to the WAN MPLS cloud;
  • Connectivity to third parties via Internet will survive, since both datacenters have physical internet uplinks;
  • Connectivity to third parties via leased lines will survive insofar those third parties are connected to both the primary and the secondary datacenter.

2: Disaster at Cegeka, rendering the secondary datacenter unusable:

  • Tier 3 virtual machines will be stopped;
  • All other services in scope will survive.

3: Disaster at Cegeka, rendering servers unusable at the primary datacenter:

This will be the same as scenario 1.

4: Disaster at Cegeka, rendering servers unusable at the secondary datacenter:

This will be the same as scenario 2.

5: Disaster at <klant>, rendering an office building unusable:

  • <klant> employees can work from home, they have full access as from the office;
  • All other services in scope will survive, insofar those services have been migrated to Cegeka’s datacenters.

In order to safeguard the survival of services, to test DR run books, and to find issues before a real disaster strikes, Cegeka and <klant> shall conduct a technical disaster recovery test once a year.

Disaster recovery testing is part of the Business Continuity Services described in the document <klant> DR plan.

Optional services

This service does not provide any optional services.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

0.0010_0030_0010_M_TM.000 Disaster recovery test (yearly) per test

Monitoring

Introduction

In support of the service delivery processes, Cegeka monitors several aspects of the different components (availability, capacity, security, database backups) via its central monitoring platform, which is based on Zenoss. All monitoring information is accessible for our engineers and for selected <klant> personnel and is visualized via a central unified web interface. Per managed system there are service windows and maintenance windows defined (this information is centrally stored and updated in the Cegeka CMDB). A link between the Cegeka CMDB and the monitoring platform ensures that the monitoring platform is always working with the latest information. This also assures that the Service Levels are very clear for both the support teams and the service delivery manager.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Monitoring of standard OS characteristics (e.g. availability, performance, security and configuration).
  • Monitoring of technical applications (e.g. Active Directory) through Zen packs, as defined at http://wiki.zenoss.org/ZenPack_Catalog.
  • Monitoring of application services, as defined by <klant>.
  • URL checks, as defined by <klant>.

Optional services

  • Setup of Cegeka’s central monitoring portal for <klant>.
  • <klant> Care Portal is accessible over the internet via the secured URL: https://ccp.cegeka.be. A username and password for logging in to the portal and providing access to the monitoring info of all assets of <klant> will be provided.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Monitoring Services

Setup and maintain a server monitoring plan including a list of relevant resources, thresholds and measuring intervals

I

R, A

Setup and maintain a URL monitoring plan including a list of relevant resources, thresholds and measuring intervals

C

R, A

Setup and maintain a services monitoring plan including a list of relevant resources, thresholds and measuring intervals

C

R, A

Set thresholds triggering alerts

 

R, A

Monitor thresholds

 

R, A

Initiate the necessary actions when thresholds are exceeded

I

R, A

Report violations of the monitoring metrics listed in the monitoring plan as monitoring tickets

I

R, A

Report capacity management issues

I

R, A

Provide access to <klant> user to monitoring portal

C

R, A

Revoke access for <klant> user to monitoring portal

C

R, A

Provide monitor reports

I

R, A

Service Part number(s)

  • 0.0050_0010_0010_A_S.000 Monitoring portal

ISAE reporting

Cegeka has built a control framework based upon its Quality Management System (QMS) and its Information Security Management System (ISMS). On a yearly basis, an independent service auditor examines Cegeka’s control framework, which is also applicable to <klant>, and expresses an opinion on the suitability of the design and the operating effectiveness of the controls to achieve Cegeka’s control objectives. The audit and the report are completed in accordance with ISAE 3000.

Such a report is typically an auditor-to-auditor report:

With regards to information security, Cegeka has extended his Quality Management System, Information Security Management System and control framework with best practices from ISO 27002. The audit of this extended control framework resulted in an ISAE 3000 report, also called an 'ISO 27002 in control statement'.

Service description

Using this service Cegeka provides <klant> with assurance about the implementation and operational effectiveness of these controls through an ISAE 3000 SOC 2 type II report provided by an independent auditor.

Cegeka includes below described processes in the ISAE 3000 SOC 2 report of the audited period, starting November 1st until October 31st of every year, taking into account at least 6 months valid data.

If Transition planning does not allow an audit on 6 months of valid data that is required to deliver a valid ISAE report, the cost of the Cegeka ISAE 3000 SOC 2 report will be charged to <klant>.

The full scope of the report covers following processes:

Process name

Objective description

Technology direction

The objective of the technology direction process is to align the user entity’s expectations with the technological opportunities and investments made or to be made, helping ensure a timely and well-considered response to changes in the competitive environment.

Project management

The objective of the project management process is to help ensure that new or significantly adjusted IT systems are made operational in an efficient, integer and effective manner.

Infrastructure development

The objective of the infrastructure development process is to help ensure that new or significantly adjusted IT systems are made operational in an efficient, integer and effective manner.

Service management

The objective of the service management process is to enable and support effective communication between Cegeka and <klant> regarding the provided Services, helping ensure the alignment between the expectations of the user entity and the service delivery.

Incident management

The objective of the incident management process is to achieve a timely and effective response to IT user incidents, allowing an increased overall productivity through quick resolution.

Request management

The objective of the request management process is to achieve a timely and effective response to IT user requests, allowing an increased overall productivity through quick processing.

Problem management

The objective of the problem management process is to improve the Service Levels, reduce the costs and enhance the overall user entity convenience and satisfaction.

Change & release management

The objective of the change & release management process is to help ensure that all Changes, including emergency maintenance and patching, related to the IT infrastructure within the production environment are formally managed through logging, assessing, authorizing, testing and approving. This ensures the stability and integrity of the production environment.

IT Asset & configuration management

The objective of the IT Asset & configuration management process is to help ensure the integrity and transparency of the IT Infrastructure, facilitating greater system availability and limiting the impact of incidents on the IT Infrastructure production environment.

Monitoring

The objective of the monitoring process is to achieve effective IT Infrastructure availability and capacity management.

Capacity management

The objective of the capacity management process is to help ensure that cost-justifiable IT capacity in all areas of IT always exists and is matched to the current and future agreed needs of the business in a timely matter.

Operations management

The objective of the operations process is to help ensure the complete and accurate batch processing of Data.

Data management

The objective of the Data management process is to help ensure that IT Infrastructure systems and business data are backed-up and that backups can be restored in a timely manner.

Information security - management

The objective of the information security process is to help ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the Data and protect the IT Assets.

Information security - logical access

The objective of the information security process is to help ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the Data and protect the IT Assets.

Information security - logical operations

The objective of the information security process is to help ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the Data and protect the IT Assets.

Information security – physical

The objective of the information security process is to help ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the Data and protect the IT Assets.

Business continuity management

The objective of the business continuity management within Cegeka is to help ensure that Cegeka has the ability to continue its business of delivering ICT services in case of a disaster.

Human resource management

The objective of the human resource management process is to manage the human capital of the organization so that the potential maximum can be deployed and rewarded.

Purchase

The objective of the purchase process is to help ensure that the services and goods, provided by third party to Cegeka, meet business requirements.

Audits

The objective of the audit process is to find out whether the Quality Management System (QMS) and the Information Security Management System (ISMS) are implemented, to review the effectiveness of the QMS and ISMS and to review whether all necessary measures are taken to manage the risks.

Information management

The objective of the information management process is to control the documents required by the Information Security Management System (ISMS) and the Quality Management System (QMS) and that these documents are qualitative, up-to-date documents and available to those who need them.

Service Part number(s)

0.0060_0010_0010_M_S.000 ISAE reporting

Software Asset Management

Introduction

The license management Service encompasses the inventory, reporting and management of the in-scope software titles, both in use at Cegeka, <klant> locations or other agreed third parties.

The Service provides XXXXX with assistance during software audit cycles of software vendors and provides an inventory of the software in use (this service is mandatory for all systems that use the SPLA and / or CSP licenses supplied by Cegeka).

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Delivery of 9 standard reports:
    • All servers;
    • All managed devices;
    • All applications, which are included in the database of the software asset management tool. Software asset management for SaaS applications is only possible when a browser is used that is supported by the software asset management tool;
    • Applications per server;
    • Applications per device;
    • Unused applications per server;
    • CMDB control report;
    • Operating systems;
    • Devices without antivirus software installed;
    • Installed software reports.
  • Detection of new licenses (Software Assets) within 30 days after installation or new releases;
  • Reporting will be done on a quarterly base included in the overall Service reporting;

Optional services

  • Providing advice towards <klant>, without any responsibility of Cegeka towards the legal audit, regarding safeguarding license compliancy including:
    • Actual licenses to the existing contract positions;
    • Optimizing the use of each IT asset.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

  • To be able to inventory servers, an agent needs to be installed. This agent needs to be able to connect to the internet at least one time per 30 days to protect against data loss.
  • The agent on the asset needs to have https connection towards itam.cegeka.be;
  • To be able to inventory a device or server, the asset needs to have a unique Cegeka CI number and therefore listed in the Cegeka CMDB;
  • Cegeka cannot be held responsible for the contents and the quality of the software recognition patterns, delivered by the tool. This Service only lists software assets, which are defined in the software pattern definitions of the tool;
  • Oracle license models are supported, but the results of the scan have no license / legal value for Oracle;
  • IBM license models are supported, but the results of the scan have no license / legal value for IBM;
  • Cegeka cannot be held responsible for claims coming out of license audits from the software vendors;
  • Service meetings are included in the already foreseen tactical governance meetings.

Microsoft SPLA licenses

Cegeka can provide Microsoft licensing to <klant>, based on SPLA. This model is a pay per use on a monthly basis model (Server OS licences (Windows or RHEL) are standard offered for the systems on the infrastructure service Compute IaaS).

Where possible according to Microsoft license policies, Cegeka can provide SPLA licenses to <klant>. A formal order form has to be signed to order or remove certain licenses.

SPLA licenses are calculated on a monthly basis. Cegeka reports and pays the number of agreed SPLA licenses towards Microsoft and invoices <klant> for these licenses. Licenses can be adjusted monthly with a 7 days prior notification (formal order or change of the order).

License compliancy is always a responsibility of <klant>. Using SPLA licenses via Cegeka does not give license compliancy guarantees for <klant> at any moment in time. It can be a part of the complete Microsoft licensing package, which <klant> has for compliancy. The Cegeka invoices may serve as compliance proof towards Microsoft but only for the SPLA licenses.

The following conditions apply:

  • In order to be able to use SPLA licenses, Cegeka must meet the Microsoft requirements. This means that servers with SPLA licenses need to be managed by Cegeka;
  • In case that software media is needed to install the correct product versions, Cegeka will inform <klant> and will acquire the needed media. Involved costs will be invoiced to <klant>;
  • Cegeka always has to comply with the Services Provider Use Rights (SPUR). The SPUR specifies use rights and conditions that apply to <klant>'s use of the products licensed under the SPLA. A customized SPUR document can be provided, but the last SPUR is always binding. These Services Provider Use Rights generally cover the most recent version of products that are available worldwide. For license terms for products that no longer appear in this edition of the Microsoft Services Provider Use Rights, a previous edition will need to be reviewed.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

General Roles and Responsibilities

Change management

C

AR

Executing standard changes (requests)

I

AR

Executing non-standard changes (requests)

I

AR

Technical platform management

I

AR

Service Part number(s)

0.0070_0010_0010_A_S.000 Usage of the SAM platform per agent

0.0070_0010_0020_A_E.000 License cost per customer (per license)

Infrastructure Management

OS Management - Windows

Introduction

The Windows OS management service includes the technical management of a Windows server – physical or virtual.

Service description

Standard services

Operating system management

This Service includes technical management of the OS of a server. Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Assuring availability of the Windows server OS;
  • Routine audits of the Windows server OS;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and proactive resolving of potential issues;
  • Documentation of system configurations;
  • Performance tuning of the Windows server OS.
  • Active management of files and disk space.

Windows Software & patch management

Patches in scope

The patches in scope for this service are related to the Windows server operating system. The following categorization is applied:

  • Critical updates
  • Security updates

Cegeka installs all patches related to ‘critical updates’ and ‘security updates’, in agreement with <klant>. All other patches are out of scope.

Cegeka implements interim patches issued via security alerts through Cegeka’s emergency change process; these may be “urgent critical” patches or patches related to an ongoing incident.

Patch frequency

The managed Windows OS service ensures that a managed server is updated to the most recent patches every month. This frequency may be different depending on specific separate agreements, this can be provided as an option in this service.

Service packs

In accordance with the Microsoft Product Support Lifecycle, the policy concerning service packs is the following:

  • When a new service pack is released, Cegeka will provide 12 months support on the previous service pack.
  • When a new service pack is released, Cegeka will inform <klant> through the agreed procedures related to the product lifecycle policy. Cegeka will also inform <klant> on the support lifecycle timeline of the new and previous service pack.

When the support of a Windows server version ends, then the support for all service packs of that version will end. In such a case, Cegeka is willing to discuss with <klant> the need to continue to provide support on a best effort basis.

Supported versions

Cegeka applies a support version lifecycle policy on the managed Windows server service, for which the Microsoft support lifecycle is used:

  • Cegeka supports all Windows server versions for which the extended support period has not yet expired. Older versions can be managed on a “best effort” basis.
  • Cegeka will inform <klant> when a new Windows version becomes available or when the extended support period expires through the agreed procedures related to the product lifecycle policy.
  • Antivirus
  • The ‘managed windows’ service ensures the security of the operating system against malware, viruses and rootkits. It also protects the managed Windows server from threats at network level. The antivirus agent handles scanning of local file systems during read and write operations.
  • Cegeka’s anti-virus service protects the managed device against security threats. The endpoint protection central environment automatically deploys updates to each agent and provides an overview over the agent status and events.
  • Optional services

Backup & restore management

This feature is the management of the OS backups and restores from an operational perspective. It is dependent on the presence of the shared backup service. This option should typically be standard for production systems but might be left out for Test/Dev Environments.

License management of the OS and underlying components

This feature is the management of the OS licenses for the managed system or components installed on the system. For this option to be available Cegeka needs certain authorizations with respect to the licenses in question, which will depend on the exact product.

Non-standard patch frequency

Specific patch frequencies can be agreed for named systems.

Custom activity list

This feature provides the possibility for <klant> to define custom activities (e.g. in the form of a workbook) to be executed by Cegeka.

Out of scope

  • The following patches are out of scope:
  • Update Rollup
  • Feature Pack
  • Update

The Cegeka technical architect assigned to <klant> discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of <klant>.

Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them and not a part of another in scope service, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process.

Management of the applications running on the windows servers is not in scope with Cegeka. In the Service Delivery Plan, specific agreements need to be documented on the installation and upgrade of applications on servers and the required permissions to do so. The following options exist:

  • Cegeka receives the installation package and a release guide and installs the application on the server;
  • <klant> or a supplier receives temporary permissions to install the application
  • An employee of <klant> or the supplier sit together with an engineer from Cegeka to do the installation/upgrade together.

Similar agreements need to be made and documented in the SDP with respect to the management of the applications on the servers, especially concerning the permissions that are required for this application management.

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the managed Windows server.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the managed Windows server.
