OS Management – Linux

Introduction

This service includes technical management of a Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) or Suse Linux Enterprise (SLES) Server operating system. Although RHEL and SLES are the standard supported Linux distributions, other distributions (like CentOS and Ubuntu) are also supported, but SLA specific exceptions must be agreed upon beforehand (e.g. the existence of maintenance contracts).

Service description

Standard services

Operating system management

This service includes technical management of the OS of a server. Technical management consists of:

  • Assuring availability of the RHEL/SLES OS;
  • Routine audits of the RHEL/SLES OS;
  • Documentation of system configurations;
  • Performance tuning of the RHEL/SLES OS;
  • Active management of files and disk space.

Linux software & patch management

Patches: Red Hat Enterprise Linux

Cegeka differentiates 2 major levels when it comes to patching RHEL:

Patches in scope

The patches in scope for this service are limited to those related to "security updates" and "OS updates". All other updates are out of scope.

Patches: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server

Patches related to the SLES operating system are categorized in:

  • Recommended
  • Security
  • Feature
  • Optional

Patch frequency

The managed Linux OS service ensures that the managed server is updated to the most recent patches every quarter.

This frequency may be different depending on specific separate agreements; this is provided as an option in this service.

Supported versions

Cegeka applies a support lifecycle policy for the Linux OS management service. It is designed to provide a clear and predictable policy for product support timelines and to assist in managing their support requirements.

Red Hat Enterprise Linux

  • Cegeka supports all RHEL server versions up to the ‘extended life phase’ (limitations related to RHEL support policies apply: https://access.redhat.com/site/support/policy/updates/errata )
  • Cegeka does not support RHEL versions in 'extended life phase', unless the operating systems is covered by an Extended Life Cycle Support Add-on contract
  • Cegeka will inform through the agreed procedures related to the product lifecycle policy when a new RHEL Version becomes available or when the extended life cycle support period will expire.

SUSE Linux Enterprise Server

  • Cegeka supports all SLES Server versions up to the ‘extended support phase’ (limitations related to SLES support policies apply: https://www.suse.com/support/policy.html)
  • Cegeka does not support SLES versions in 'extended support phase’, unless the SLES operating system is covered by a Long Term Service Pack Support contract.
  • Cegeka will inform <klant> through the agreed procedures related to the product lifecycle policy when a new SLES version becomes available or when the extended life cycle support period will expire.

OS updates/service packs

Cegeka conducts an annual OS update for RHEL managed servers, as these OS Updates have a greater impact on the application level.

In accordance with the SUSE Product Support Lifecycle, the policy concerning service packs is the following:

  • When a new service pack is released, Cegeka will provide 6 months support on the previous service pack excluding SLES managed operating systems covered by a Long Term Service Pack Support Contract;
  • When a new Service Pack is released, Cegeka will inform through the agreed procedures related to the Product Lifecycle Policy. Cegeka will also inform on the Support Lifecycle Timeline of the new and previous Service Pack.

Monitoring

Monitoring is focused on status and availability monitoring and includes:

  • Measurement of system parameters of the <klant>’s IT infrastructure on operating system level;
  • Verification of availability of services and processes. (Detailed expectations need to be agreed separately with <klant>);
  • Notification via e-mail and/or sms when exceeding predefined thresholds and predefined statuses;
  • Visualization of monitoring results (events) via a central monitoring portal.

The list of standard monitoring settings and the list of monitored system parameters are documented in a standard monitoring plan. This is used as a basis for a customized monitoring plan to fit the needs of <klant>. In this service, management of monitoring datacenter infrastructure components is included. The service components are:

  • Monitoring of servers;
  • Monitoring of infrastructure software;
  • Portal servers.

<klant> has an insight in the outcome of monitoring by means of monthly reports. An external monitoring portal can be provided as an option to <klant>.

Optional services

Backup & restore management

This feature is the management of the OS backups and restores from an operational perspective. It is dependent on the presence of the Shared Backup Service. This option should typically be standard for production systems but might be left out for Test/Dev environments.

License management of the OS and underlying components

This feature is the management of the OS licenses for the managed system or components installed on the system. For this option to be available Cegeka needs certain authorizations with respect to the licenses in question, which will depend on the exact product.

Non-standard patch frequency

Specific patch frequencies can be agreed for named systems.

Antivirus

On the Linux Operating System, antivirus and antimalware is not included and not installed by default. The service should be added as an exception on Linux servers that serves as a SAMBA file server or mail server where antivirus and antimalware is implemented.

Custom activity list

This feature provides the possibility for <klant> to define custom activities (e.g. in the form of a workbook) to be executed by Cegeka.

Out of scope

<klant> assigned Cegeka technical architect discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of <klant>.

Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process.

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the Managed Linux Server.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the Managed Linux Server.

Management of the applications running on the Linux servers is not in scope with Cegeka. In the Service Delivery Plan, specific agreements need to be documented on the installation and upgrade of applications on servers and the required permissions to do so. The following options exist:

  • Cegeka receives the installation package and a release guide and installs the application on the server;
  • <klant> or a supplier receives temporary permissions to install the application;
  • An employee of <klant> or the supplier sit together with an engineer from Cegeka to do the installation/upgrade together;

Similar agreements need to be made and documented in the SDP with respect to the management of the applications on the servers, especially concerning the permissions that are required for this application management.

OS Management – AIX

Introduction

The IBM Advanced Interactive executive OS management service includes the technical management of an AIX instance on a physical machine or on a LPAR.

Service description

Standard services

Operating System management

This service includes technical management of the OS of a server. Technical management consists of:

  • Assuring availability of the AIX OS;
  • Routine audits of the AIX OS;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and proactive resolving of potential issues;
  • Documentation of system configurations;
  • Performance tuning of the AIX OS;
  • Active management of files and disk space.

The management of LPARs through an HMC is included in the service. The management of the HMC itself is billed separately.

Software & patch management

Yearly the AIX partitions are upgraded to the IBM recommended technology level.

Monitoring

Monitoring is focused on status and availability monitoring and includes:

  • Measurement of system parameters of the <klant>’s IT infrastructure on Operating System level.
  • Verification of availability of services and processes. (Detailed expectations need to be agreed separately with the <klant>)
  • Notification via e-mail and/or SMS when exceeding predefined thresholds and predefined statuses;
  • Visualization of monitoring results (events and trends) via a central monitoring portal.

The list of standard monitoring settings and the list of monitored system parameters are documented in a standard monitoring plan. This is used as a basis for a customized monitoring plan to fit the needs of the <klant>. In this service, management of monitoring datacenter infrastructure components is included. These components are:

  • Monitoring of servers;
  • Monitoring of infrastructure software;
  • Portal servers.

<klant> has insight in the outcome of monitoring by means of monthly reports and via the monitoring portal.

Backup & restore management

OS backups and restores from an operational perspective is provided via the shared backup service. The Linux server is classified according to the backup policy plan.

Non-standard patch frequency

Specific patch frequencies can be agreed for named systems.

Custom activity list

This feature provides the possibility for the <klant> to define custom activities (e.g. in the form of a workbook) to be executed by Cegeka.

Out of scope

The <klant> assigned Cegeka technical architect discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of the <klant>.

Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process.

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the Managed AIX Server.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the Managed AIX Server.
  • Part number
  • O.0130_0010_0010_C_S.000 Physical and Virtual - Ux Gold
  • O.0130_0010_0020_C_S.000 Physical and Virtual - Ux Silver

Responsibilities

See paragraph 4.2 OS Management – Linux

OS Management – HP-UX (optional)

Introduction

The Hewlett Packard Unix OS management service includes the technical management of an HP-UX instance on a physical machine or a VPAR or a NPAR.

Service description

Standard services

Operating System management

This service includes technical management of the OS of a server. Technical management consists of:

  • Assuring availability of the HP-UX OS;
  • Routine audits of the HP-UX OS;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and proactive resolving of potential issues;
  • Documentation of system configurations;
  • Performance tuning of the HP-UX OS;
  • Active management of files and disk space.

Software & patch management

Yearly, the HP-UX OS is upgraded to the latest Operation Environment, which is at least 6 months old. Appropriate patches on the Operation Environment are, after consultation with HP, installed during the patch round.

Monitoring

Monitoring is focused on status and availability monitoring and includes:

  • Measurement of system parameters of the Customer’s IT infrastructure on Operating System level.
  • Verification of availability of services and processes. (Detailed expectations need to be agreed separately with the Customer)
  • Notification via e-mail and/or SMS when exceeding predefined thresholds and predefined statuses;
  • Visualization of monitoring results (events and trends) via a central monitoring portal.

The list of standard monitoring settings and the list of monitored system parameters are documented in a standard monitoring plan. This is used as a basis for a customized monitoring plan to fit the needs of the Customer. In this service, management of monitoring datacenter infrastructure components is included. These components are:

  • Monitoring of servers;
  • Monitoring of infrastructure software;
  • Portal servers.

The Customer has an insight in the outcome of monitoring by means of monthly reports. An external monitoring portal can be provided as an option to the Customer.

Optional services

Backup & Restore Management

This feature is the management of the OS backups and restores from an operational perspective. It is dependent on the presence of the Shared Backup Service. This option should typically be standard for production systems but might be left out for Test/Dev Environments.

License Management of the OS and underlying components

This feature is the management of the OS licenses for the Managed System or components installed on the system. For this option to be available Cegeka needs certain authorizations with respect the licenses in question, which will depend on the exact product.

Non-standard patch frequency

Specific patch frequencies can be agreed for named systems.

Custom activity list

This feature provides the possibility for the Customer to define custom activities (e.g. in the form of a workbook) to be executed by Cegeka.

Out of scope

The Customer assigned Cegeka technical architect discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of the Customer.

Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process.

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the Managed HP-UX Server.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the Managed HP-UX Server.

Part number

O.0130_0010_0010_C_S.000 Ux Gold

O.0130_0010_0020_C_S.000 Ux Silver

O.0130_0010_0030_C_S.000 Ux Bronze

OS Management – Power Hosting

Introduction

This Service manages all aspects related to <klant>’s iSeries implementation hosted at Cegeka.

Service description

Standard services

System iOS management

  • Assuring availability of the OS corresponding to <klant>’s Service Level;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and resolving of issues;
  • Documentation of system configurations;
  • Performance tuning of the OS;
  • Active management of files and disk space;
  • License management of the OS and underlying components.

Monitoring

This feature is focused on status and availability monitoring and includes:

  • Measurement of system parameters of <klant>’s iSeries OS;
  • Verification of availability of services, processes and ports of the <klant> applications. Detailed requirements need to be agreed upon separately with <klant>, and will be documented in the monitoring plan;
  • Notification via e-mail and/or SMS when exceeding predefined thresholds and predefined statuses;
  • Visualization of monitoring results (events and trends) via a central monitoring portal.

The list of standard monitoring settings and the list of monitored system parameters are documented in a standard monitoring plan. This is used as a basis for a customized monitoring plan to fit the needs of <klant>.

<klant> has an insight in the outcome of monitoring by means of monthly reports. An external monitoring portal can be provided as an option to <klant>.

Software & patch management

  • Implement interim patches issued via security alerts through Cegeka’s emergency change process;
  • Implement patches addressing specific issues for The <klant> (after agreement with the <klant>, via Cegeka’s emergency change process);
  • Each Quarter the Systems are fully patched.

This service doesn’t include:

  • The configuration and/or activation of additional features;
  • Necessary changes and project activities caused by patch management;
  • Software updates for which it’s necessary to change the application and/or reconfigure related systems with which the application interacts.

Backup & Restore management

This feature contains:

  • Backup and Restore of iSeries will be documented in the backup plan as part of the Document for Agreements and Procedures (DAP).
  • Management of the backup solution to a dedicated tape robot
  • Offsite storage of the tapes (via courier)
  • Management of the backup schemes
  • Restore on request

Physical iSeries Management

This feature contains:

  • Management of the hardware support contracts
  • Firmware patch management

Optional features

This service does not offer any additional features

Out of scope

The <klant> assigned Cegeka technical architect discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of the <klant>.

Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process.

Conditions

The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the Managed iSeries Server.

The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the Managed iSeries Server.

Part number

O.0140_0010_0010_M_S.000 SPH platform management - Shared Power Hosting - platform management
O.0300_0010_0010_A_S.000 Single Datacenter - Virtual Server Resources (IBM i / IOS) - IBM i; sytem unit single datacenter / 76 CPW / 1 GB Ram / unlimited i5 OS V7Rx + user licenses / BU agent License (BRMS) / (minimum 7 units per partition required)

O.0320_0060_0010_A_S.000 Single Datacenter - Storage (IBM i / IOS) - Managed SAN capacity / GB (single storage system/datacenter) - High End (FS900 Flash) (S-T0) (Min 200 GiB + extra 50 GiB blocks)

O.0340_0060_0010_A_S.000 Backup (IBM i / IOS) - LPAR back-up - per GB used (compressed) space on back-up storage device (EMC² data domain) (Min 200 GiB + extra 50 GiB blocks) - Storage Replication

Hypervisor Management

Introduction

The Hypervisor management service includes the technical management of a Hypervisor on physical environment.

Service description

Standard services

This Service includes technical management of the Hypervisor. Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Hardware management
    • CPU/RAM
    • Physical NICs
    • HBAs / FC adapters / iSCSI adapter
    • Physical storage
    • iLO
  • Managing hypervisor Hosts and Clusters represents supporting:
    • hypervisor Clusters (including SCVMM, Hyper-V Manager, Admin Center, vSphere vCenter, XenServer, XenCenter)
    • host licensing
    • availability, capacity and performance
    • Datastores / Cluster Shared Volumes (VMDK /RDM / VHDX disks)
    • High availability
    • host profiles
    • Virtual switches
    • time settings
    • SNMP settings
    • resource pools
    • VM Resources
    • VM options
    • Startup/shutdown options
    • VMware tools / XenServer tools / Hyper-V integration services
    • Roles and accounts
    • Auditing
    • AD authentication
    • Security
    • snapshots, checkpoints and machine images
    • Power management

The following activities are part of this service Security component:

  • Manage lockdown mode
  • Manage security certificates
  • Manage Single Sign On
  • Manage network security policies
  • Secure storage migration traffic
  • Manage guarded fabrics
  • Manage shielded VMs
  • Quarterly patch management
  • Monitoring

Supported technologies

This service supports the following products:

  • VMware vSphere ESXi and vCenter
  • Microsoft Hyper-V and SCVMM
  • Citrix XenServer and XenCenter

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

O.0120_0040_0140_A_S.000 Hyperconverged management (incl. Hypervisor)

Technical application management

Introduction

The Technical Application Management (TAM) service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the customer business applications from a technical perspective. Technical Application Management is categorized in three levels:

  • Basic Technical Application Management services that have as focus to keep the business application and middleware up-and-running;
  • Advanced Technical Application Management services build upon the basic Services and include tasks to improve the applications technical parameters;
  • Premium Technical Application Management services are separate Services on top of the advanced Services, which provide further improvements, which are tailored to specific products.

The enablement of these three levels of TAM services is on a per business application basis.

Service description

Basic technical application management

Cegeka takes full responsibility for the technical application landscape. Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following activities including all dependency components needed to have a business application up and running, i.e. IIS, J2EE or similar components (i.e. middleware or related applications).

TAM service desk support

Incidents and requests about the business applications are registered by Cegeka service desk agents. If possible, Cegeka service desk handles the incident or request using standardized procedures. Otherwise, it is transferred to second line engineers, who have a thorough knowledge about the application.

Incidents shall be registered, based on customer calls or on events generated by Cegeka’s technical monitoring of the application status and availability.

Incidents and requests are handled within the contractually agreed Service Levels. Actions in response to customer end-user calls and alerts originating from the Cegeka monitoring services are started. Tickets, which can be handled using standard procedures, are solved. The remaining tickets are handed over to engineering support.

Recurring maintenance

Most business applications require that a number of well-defined tasks are executed on a regular basis in order to ensure the availability of the business application and the system resources it uses. Examples include log rotation and connection pool clean-up. This Service ensures that the necessary tasks are executed at appropriate intervals.

Predictable incidents are avoided. Recurring maintenance activities are executed in accordance with the maintenance plan.

Engineering support

Incidents and requests that cannot be handled by Cegeka service desk by applying standardized procedures are

transferred to second line engineers. These engineers have application-specific knowledge necessary to handle these incidents with the goal of resolving them within the Service Levels as specified by their priority.

The service activity ensures the application’s technical availability is restored as per the Service Level Agreement in the event of technical failure. This doesn’t provide any guarantees on the level of functional availability and is merely allowing the functional application owner to operate and/or troubleshoot the functional application service it hosts.

In case of structural incidents, a problem ticket is created. In case an incident cannot be solved, but a work- around is available, a problem ticket is created in order to realize a structural resolution for the incident. As problems possibly lead to structural Changes in the application or infrastructure, the investigation into the problems’ cause is not in scope of this Service. This is either handled through the advanced Service or by a third party.

  • Ensure standard requests and incidents are handled within Service Levels;
  • Requests and incidents which could not be handled by service desk and have been dispatched to engineering are handled;
  • Repeating incidents are handed over to the problem handling service.

Conditions

Business application maintenance contracts must be in place for those activities outside the scope of Cegeka,

including third line support on specific Business Applications, and development/coding of business applications

Application patch deployment

The service activity ensures patches are deployed onto the technical application as per the predefined patch management plan or upon explicit request of customer. customer is accountable for both timings and instructions (responsibility for instructions can reside with the product vendor). By default critical (security) patches and patch sets are included in the patch management plan.

Deployment is limited to applying the patches to the technical application as is, excluding configuration changes and activation of functionality added by the patch(es) or patch set(s). The latter is done via RFC containing content, scope and exceptions. This RFC acts as patch deployment plan.

Customer remains in the lead by determining either the scope and high level planning or by explicitly requesting one off patch deployments unless patch planning & management is handled by Cegeka as part of the advanced service package.

By default, Cegeka performs two patch deployment rounds per year. Within such a patch deployment round, deployments are phased over the different environments (e.g. acceptance is patched before production). Systems within an environments are patched in bulk (e.g. all production systems).

  • Patches are deployed in accordance with the patch management plan;
  • Patches in patch list are applied on the applications in scope, according to the planning.

Technical monitoring

Applications make use of various system resources, such as memory and CPU. These resources assigned need to be balanced in order to allow all applications on the system to work correctly. In this service, the resource assignment to a business application is measured in order to ensure that the resource utilization stays within a predefined range.

In case a business application requires a number of jobs to be executed automatically, this Service also ensures that the jobs are executed and that incidents are handled that occur if one of the jobs cannot be executed. This Service only concerns jobs that are executed by the application itself. In case the jobs are handled by a job

scheduler, the job execution is part of the management of the job scheduler.

  • Resources are measured and verified as defined in the monitoring plan;
  • When thresholds are exceeded the necessary actions are initiated via service desk and/or engineering support, for the avoidance of doubt, monitoring notification will be emailed automatically to the responsible resolver customer group;
  • The monitoring status information is published externally via the Cegeka monitoring portal.

Advanced technical application management Services

Problem handling

In case a problem is created for one or more incidents, this Service investigates how the incident(s) can be solved in a structural manner and can be avoided in the future. The solution will be implemented through the RFC process.

Only problems that concern technical issues are in scope of this service. In case of functional issues, the problem must be investigated by functional application managers.

  • Avoidance of incidents due to known problems;
  • Improvement in service availability;
  • Creation of problem ticket(s);
  • Investigation and definition of root cause and proposed solution alternatives;
  • Categorization of the problem ticket agreed upon by both customer and Cegeka:
  • “known error”: problem is recognized and accepted;
  • “work-around”: problem is bypassed using a work-around;
  • “structural solution”: problem is solved in a structural way.
  • For each problem the outcome may consist of the following (depending on the categorization):
  • Technical level: one or more RFC’s are created to resolve the underlying cause of the problem;
  • Process & procedure level: updates to process(es) and procedure(s) are defined and communicated to customer and within Cegeka.
  • Reports as part of the monthly service review:
  • Problem overview as part of the service status report;
  • Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and Service Improvement Report (SIR) for P1 problems.

Backup planning & management

Responsibility for planning, realization and impact analysis for application backup lies with Cegeka. The backup planning & management service ensures the backup plan is defined and maintained,

whereas backup job execution – part of technical application management basic services - focuses on correct execution of backups in accordance with the backup plan and SLA. The goal is to ensure that backups are created to meet the backup requirements in the most optimal way.

Whereas the initial creation of the backup plan is prior to daily operation and thus part of transition, application landscapes change (growth, changing service level requirements, changed features). This provides opportunities to creating backups more efficiently, while still meeting customer’s functional service level requirements.

Servers are backed-up to match the functional backup requirements in the most optimal and efficient way.

Application patch planning & management

Similar to backup job execution and backup planning & management, patch planning & management is the intelligent service of critically reviewing available patches, determining relevance and possible impact within the specific customer context resulting in a more selective patch deployment, rather than predefined bulk deployment. The benefit of this service is that customer is provided with a relevant subset of available patches

reducing downtime and adverse effects on the application’s operation.

Although this Service provides analysis and guidance at the level of patches, it remains the responsibility of customer to decide upon actual deployment and timings.

  • The most optimal balance between risk exposure on one hand and patch deployment risk and effort on the other is achieved.
  • The patching plan represents the patch scope and relevant to customer’s context.
  • Optimizations to the patch plan are defined, developed and implemented (after approval by customer).

Job management

This Services deals with the addition of new jobs and maintenance of existing jobs in the internal application scheduler, based upon the content and scheduling requirements, provided by the functional application manager.

The technical application managers ensure that the new job does not conflict with existing jobs and is scheduled in the most optimal way.

Optionally, in order to optimize the job schedule, the execution of individual jobs can be monitored and start times can be adjusted. This way, additional load on the system (e.g. if the number of transactions increases) can be compensated.

  • Scheduled jobs are executed in compliance with customer’s functional requirements and run without conflicts with other jobs
  • Optimizations to the job schedules are identified, developed and implemented (after approval by customer via an RFC)

Functional monitoring

The Service implements and executes a combination of distinct parameter checks and pre-defined functional scenarios that are indicative of the application health. The parameters and scenarios are defined in collaboration between Cegeka and customer and are by default limited to the possibilities of the standard built-in tooling and Cegeka’s default monitoring platform. Although the management of specialized monitoring tools is not in scope of this Service, they can be used if made available separately.

The results are, dependent on the application, typically reported in happy pages or dashboards. Incidents will be acted upon through the scripted execution of standard procedures or via an escalation to second line support.

The functional availability of the application is monitored and disruptions of the functional availability are acted upon in a timely manner according to SLA of incident management. When functional checks are violated the necessary actions are initiated via service desk and/or engineering support the Cegeka Customer Care Portal can be used to publish the monitoring status information unless application specific tooling is used.

Premium technical application management Services

The below Services can be contracted separately per application:

Application Hardening

Application hardening is the service activity pursuing the application’s security configuration meets business requirements. As a first step, the security configuration parameters are screened, the optimal settings are determined and documented, based upon public guidelines and standards. Upon deployment the hardened configuration is applied to the application and maintained during its further operational life cycle.

Together with the hardening process, possible exceptions are well documented to keep trace of deviations from the original/standard, both at the level of content and approval(s).

Using the configuration compliance scanning service, the hardening of the systems will be checked and guaranteed.

  • The technical attack surface of the application is a small as possible within the specific customer context
  • The hardened application state is documented
  • The application is hardened to comply with the hardening state objective
  • Exceptions to the hardening state objective are discussed and agreed upon with customer as well as documented
  • Reports as part of the monthly service review:
    • An overview of hardening state vs. hardening state objective per target environment

Performance monitoring

In this Service, the performance of the application is assessed using built-in functionality or vendor provided tools. The metrics measured include (but are not limited to), depending on the type of application, response times and resource usage.

  • The usage of specialized performance monitoring tools are out of scope of this Service.
  • The application performance complies with the performance requirements as defined by customer.
  • When performance thresholds are violated the necessary actions are initiated via the service desk and/or engineering support services.

Technical database management

Introduction

The Cegeka database management service consists of the activities related to the <klant> database environment as described in this section. Cegeka provides database management services for Oracle Database, Microsoft SQL Server and MySQL. The following definitions apply:

  • A database server or RDBMS: logical set of software, resources and processes required to make one or more databases operationally available;
  • A database: a set of structured data that is used as one logical, functional whole. A database consists of data, data structures and relational structures required to use the data in a logical and consistent way.

The table below provides an overview of the definitions above applied to the different database products:

PRODUCT

DATABASE SERVER

DATABASE (*)

ORACLE DATABASE

RDBMS or instance

Schema

MICROSOFT SQL SERVER

Server

Database

ORACLE MYSQL

Server

Database

(*) From the perspective of the application, a logical database can consist of one or more technical schemas/databases.

Service description

Standard services

  • TAM basic services

Optional services

  • Hardening

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met, with the exception of the conditions, mentioned in the Technical Application Management Service description.

I, C

R, A

The existing description applies to the service database management standard. Where an application or business application is spoken, this can be read as a database or business database.

TAM service desk support

Incidents and requests about the business applications are registered by Cegeka service desk agents. If possible, Cegeka service desk handles the incident or request using standardized procedures. Otherwise, it is transferred to second line engineers, who have a thorough knowledge about the application.

Incidents shall be registered, based on <klant> calls or on events generated by Cegeka’s technical monitoring of the application status and availability.

Incidents and requests are handled within the contractually agreed Service Levels. Actions in response to <klant> end-user calls and alerts originating from the Cegeka monitoring services are started. Tickets, which can be handled using standard procedures, are solved. The remaining tickets are handed over to engineering support.

Recurring maintenance

Most business applications require that a number of well-defined tasks are executed on a regular basis in order to ensure the availability of the business application and the system resources it uses. Examples include log rotation and connection pool clean-up. This Service ensures that the necessary tasks are executed at appropriate intervals.

Predictable incidents are avoided. Recurring maintenance activities are executed in accordance with the maintenance plan.

R, A

C

Engineering support

Incidents and requests that cannot be handled by Cegeka service desk by applying standardized procedures are transferred to second line engineers. These engineers have application-specific knowledge necessary to handle these incidents with the goal of resolving them within the Service Levels as specified by their priority.

The service activity ensures the application’s technical availability is restored as per the Service Level Agreement in the event of technical failure. This doesn’t provide any guarantees on the level of functional availability and is merely allowing the functional application owner to operate and/or troubleshoot the functional application service it hosts.

In case of structural incidents, a problem ticket is created. In case an incident cannot be solved, but a work- around is available, a problem ticket is created in order to realize a structural resolution for the incident. As problems possibly lead to structural Changes in the application or infrastructure, the investigation into the problems’ cause is not in scope of this Service. This is either handled through the advanced Service or by a third party.

  • Ensure standard requests and incidents are handled within Service Levels;
  • Requests and incidents which could not be handled by service desk and have been dispatched to engineering are handled;
  • Repeating incidents are handed over to the problem handling service.

Conditions

  • Business application maintenance contracts must be in place for those activities outside the scope of Cegeka,
  • including third line support on specific Business Applications, and development/coding of business applications

Responsibilities production and acceptance

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Application patch deployment

The service activity ensures patches are deployed onto the technical application as per the predefined patch management plan or upon explicit request of <klant>. <klant> is accountable for both timings and instructions (responsibility for instructions can reside with the product vendor). By default critical (security) patches and patch sets are included in the patch management plan.

Deployment is limited to applying the patches to the technical application as is, excluding configuration changes and activation of functionality added by the patch(es) or patch set(s). The latter is done via RFC containing content, scope and exceptions. This RFC acts as patch deployment plan.

<klant> remains in the lead by determining either the scope and high level planning or by explicitly requesting one off patch deployments unless patch planning & management is handled by Cegeka as part of the advanced service package.

By default, Cegeka performs two patch deployment rounds per year. Within such a patch deployment round, deployments are phased over the different environments (e.g. acceptance is patched before production). Systems within an environment are patched in bulk (e.g. all production systems).

  • Patches are deployed in accordance with the patch management plan;
  • Patches in patch list are applied on the applications in scope, according to the planning.

Responsibilities production and acceptance

Technical monitoring

Applications make use of various system resources, such as memory and CPU. These resources assigned need to be balanced in order to allow all applications on the system to work correctly. In this service, the resource assignment to a business application is measured in order to ensure that the resource utilization stays within a predefined range.

In case a business application requires a number of jobs to be executed automatically, this Service also ensures that the jobs are executed and that incidents are handled that occur if one of the jobs cannot be executed. This Service only concerns jobs that are executed by the application itself. In case the jobs are handled by a job scheduler, the job execution is part of the management of the job scheduler.

  • Resources are measured and verified as defined in the monitoring plan;
  • When thresholds are exceeded the necessary actions are initiated via service desk and/or engineering support, for the avoidance of doubt, monitoring notification will be emailed automatically to the responsible resolver <klant> group;
  • The monitoring status information is published externally via the Cegeka monitoring portal.

Responsibilities production and acceptance

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Infrastructure Services

Compute IaaS

Introduction

Cegeka’s managed service, to provide and support virtual machines, is based on VMware. Our private cloud services provide virtual servers in different types: Tier 1, Tier 2 and Tier 3. In case of a datacenter failure, a VMware Site Recovery Manager (SRM) will move the virtual machines of type Tier 1 to the other datacenter.

The setup of the private infrastructure consists out of blades that are distributed across our datacenters. Each blade is added to one of the high-availability clusters within a datacenter. If a blade server fails due to a disruption, the impacted virtual machines (VMs) are migrated automatically onto another blade server within the same cluster.

The virtual cloud platform contains virtual machines in three different Tiers:

  • Tier 1 VM: The Tier 1 environment is used for virtual servers that always need to be available, even in case of disaster. If a datacenter would become unavailable, Cegeka will activate its Disaster Recovery Procedure using predefined plans. Once the DRP procedure has been executed, the virtual server will be operational in another datacenter with the same specifications as before.
  • Tier 2 VM: This VM can be used for any environment, which does not have DR requirements.
  • Tier 3 VM: This VM uses capacity reserved for DR in the other datacenter. Therefore, Tier 3 contains servers that will be shut down in case of disaster recovery to free up resources for Tier 1 VMs.

Service description

Standard Services

  • Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:
  • Virtual Hardware
  • Microsoft Windows Core Infrastructure server license (SPLA) of the virtual system;
  • RHEL (Red Hat Enterprise Linux) support subscription of the virtual system;
  • Management of all underlying hardware components;
  • All license costs related to hypervisor and underlying hardware;
  • Management of the storage data stores;
  • Patch management of the underlying physical hardware;
  • Patch management of the hypervisor software;
  • 24x7 management of the hypervisor environment.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

A new VM might be refused as Standard Change when the number of the requested vCPU and vRAM is considered too high. Currently the limitations are set to 8 vCPU and 32Gb RAM. To qualify for a Standard Change. Alternative sizing is possible, but will require specific action and a longer implementation time.

Tier 3 VM’s are only available for <klant> who are using Tier 1 VM’s 90% of the allocated Tier 1 memory capacity is available for Tier 3 VM’s.

Responsibilities

I

Service Part number(s)

Storage NAS

Introduction

The shared object storage service provides a platform.

Providing CAS archiving capabilities:

  • To store large amounts of cloud native application data and/or infrequently accessed data safely and economically.
  • To store data on a geo redundant, scalable enterprise storage solution (geo-redundancy: Belgium (Hasselt):
    • The Netherlands (Geleen).
  • From which you can access data geo redundantly via:
    • RESTful API
    • S3 (fully compliant)
    • CIFS
    • NFS
  • That is HIPAA and SEC 17a-4 compliant, which ensures the <klant> of data consistency at all times. European counter parts are Data Protection Directive 95/46/EC and GDPR.
  • Which has no single point of failures and protects its data via erasure coding over multiple geographical locations.

The Shared Object Storage Service manages the components of the Object Storage platform infrastructure. These components are:

  • The object storage data serving nodes;
  • The object storage;
  • CIFS agents.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • CAS data archiving solution
  • Access via all listed access protocols
  • Data traffic (in/out)
  • Object creation
  • Object access/retrieval
  • Object deletion
  • Life cycle transitions
  • Custom metadata
  • Metadata search capabilities
  • Replicated data by default

Optional services

The following options can only be deployed after technical review:

  • Openstack/Swift
  • HDFS (Hadoop)

Conditions

  • Performance: note that this service has a performance, which is similar to “internet-speed”. The storage is not intended as a fast storage for files and objects. Compare this to Amazon S3 and Glacier storage.
  • Not a drop in replacement for NAS: while the service can offer NAS functionality, it is not intended as a NAS replacement. The NAS access is only intended as an additional interface to the data.
  • NFS: Supported NAS functionality is NFSv3.
  • CIFS: CIFS is based on a client to be installed on a server. The compatibilities matrix of the CIFS agent is leading for which systems are supported for being the CIFS front-end.
  • S3 API: The system is fully compliant with the S3 API with the exception of a number of functions
  • Search/Metadata: Metadata searches are possible. There is a limit on the number of metadata fields that can be used.
  • All use cases could use the object storage technology from this service but the service does not include the full functionality of all use cases in the list. For some additional services should be aligned.

Responsibilities

<klant>

R, A

Introduction

This Service offers the ability to connect dedicated hardware to the Cegeka cloud storage (SAN Storage).

For <klant> this service will be used for the physical system where the Client operating systems will run.

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • The SAN switches and cabling;

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • Service can only be used in combination with the Block level SAN Storage.
  • The operating system using the storage must natively support multi-patching or support the software provided by Cegeka.
  • The physical servers must have compatible fiber channel adapter cards to connect to the SAN network.

O.0320_0100_0010_A_S.000 Server connectivity Rack - SAN ports

SAN & storage device management

Introduction

The SAN & storage device management service is a Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, administration and planning of all functions for Storage systems.

Service description

Standard features

The following standard features are included in this Service:

General activities:

  • Monitoring and controlling storage performance according to data management policies[1]
  • Monitoring storage constantly and escalating data growth when it reaches a critical point to ensure timely procurement of sufficient storage.
  • Proposing a data management plan to manage data growth and capacity
  • Reports on performance and capacity

Daily operations and administration:

  • Storage Provisioning & Management
  • Defines physical and logical layout (arrays, groups, volumes, pools, LUNs)
  • Creates new LUNs
  • Removes LUNs
  • Creates new Shares
  • Removes Shares
  • Manages host mappings (hosts, host groups, host types)
  • Backs up Device configuration
  • Masks Devices to HBA
  • Manages unused and used resources (disks, ports, controllers)
  • Determines failed hardware components (disk drives, power supplies, fans, controllers, ports)
  • Coordinates array capacity decision making
  • Coordinates disk types decision making
  • Troubleshoots Hardware issues
  • Logs Hardware issues with vendors
  • Manages Volume size (grow/shrink)
  • Manages volume location/relocation/migration within the same array or between arrays
  • Migrates RAID levels
  • Reports on number of disks available and used[2];
  • Remote Updates Microcode / firmware (our recommendation is that all these activities are done onsite)”
  • Documents Trend reporting on resource usage
  • Performs Storage space validations (trend analysis)
  • Configures replication
  • Manages deduplication and compression
  • Manages disk alignment
  • Storage Performance Management
  • Performs Configuration Monitoring
  • Performs Logs & Alert monitoring
  • Manages Performance Monitoring and Tuning
  • Manages Performance Analysis (trend analysis)
  • Storage maintenance
  • Monitors and Troubleshoots Proactively
  • Follows up and interacts with Vendor
  • Implements and coordinates Array Vendor Best Practices
  • Designs Scalability
  • Fabric Switch Management
  • Configures Domain Parameters
  • Configures interfaces on SAN Switch
  • Configures fiber channel aliases for new host
  • Configures & Managing VSANs
  • Configures high availability on controller level
  • Configures Inter-Switch links
  • Configures Port-channels / Trucking
  • Configures & Manages Zones
  • Configures Fiber channel routing services & protocols
  • Configures users & common rules
  • Configures System message logging
  • Configures IP Storage
  • Configures FCIP & iSCSI
  • Performs Problem Determination and Resolution
  • Fabric switch monitoring
  • Proactively Monitors and Troubleshoots
  • Configures SNMP alerts & monitoring
  • Troubleshoots & diagnoses Fabric & Switch Issues (Hardware & Software)
  • Performs Impact & Performance analysis
  • Storage Volume & File System Provisioning
  • Allocates / De-allocates Volumes/Logical disks
  • Manages Nomenclature for Volumes
  • Performs Volume Management (Grow/Shrink)
  • Provisions & designs new disks
  • Performs LUN Management/ Exports Management
  • Adds and Removes Disks
  • Performs Cluster Configuration & Management
  • Monitors and resolves issues with the synchronization to a secondary SAN
  • Stops Synchronization as requested
  • Reinstates Synchronization as requested
  • Storage Snapshot Management
  • Performs Replication and Recovery
  • Performs Snapshot Management
  • Coordinates Hardware Maintenance & Support

Scope

The scope of the San & Storage management service is one disk array.

Optional feature to this service

  • N/A

Dependencies

Mandatory: “Service Desk” (2nd line)

Location

The service can be delivered for all Client locations and the Cegeka datacenters.

Ownership

The equipment is being owned by <klant> (or in a lease construction)

Responsibility

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Dedicated LAN Management

 

 

Technical platform management

I

R, A

Change management

C

R, A

Executing standard requests

I

R, A

Executing non-standard requests

I

R, A

On-site interventions outside Cegeka datacenter

 A, I

R, I

Service Part number(s)

O.0320_0090_0010_M_TM.000 SAN & storage device management

Backup

Introduction

Cegeka’s backup solution uses a disk based backup solution. Disk based backups of all data shall be stored cross-site from the site containing the data. Monthly, the production backup is offloaded to tape and shipped to external storage. The monthly backups are stored on disk for one month and on tape for one year. Yearly, the backup will be stored for a period of ten years.

The backup infrastructure at Cegeka has a high scalability, availability and performance:

  • Dedicated 10Gb switch infrastructure for the backup environment.
  • A four-node storage cluster in each of the Cegeka datacenters.
  • Possibility to extend each cluster to up to 16 petabytes.
  • Possibility of using tiered backup storage.

The Service provides three methods of ‘off-site’ storage:

  • Cross-site backup to another datacenter of Cegeka.
  • Replication to another datacenter of Cegeka.
  • Offloading to tape media combined with shipping of tapes outside the datacenter.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Execute backups and restores for supported operating systems.
    • The backup plan contains all details regarding backup agreed between <klant> and Cegeka (backup schedules). There is a clear relation between each relevant Configuration Item (CI) and the backup schedules.
  • Backup characteristics:
    • The system and data will be backed up according to backup methodology, retention schemes and schedule as documented in the backup plan.
    • Failed backups will be re-executed. In case of consecutive failures an incident ticket will be created;
    • Certain data can be excluded from backup.
    • As a part of the normal backup strategy (e.g. database ‘files’ are excluded as databases will be backed up with another agent which is not file based).
    • Explicitly agreed between <klant> and Cegeka.
    • Both cases are documented in the backup plan.
  • Restore characteristics:
    • Systems and data will be restored based on the used backup methods and data availability (retention) as defined in the backup plan.
    • Restore operations will be executed on request and need to be specified in detail: full system restore, (partial) data restore, destination. These restores will always be billed on a time and material basis (not included in the service charges).
  • All necessary backup infrastructures within the Cegeka datacenters:
    • Backup servers;
    • Media servers;
    • Backup software and licenses (including client software and license when applicable).
  • Data storage.
  • Image based backup (block based backup);

Optional services

  • In addition to cross-site backup, backup replication to secondary datacenter of Cegeka.
  • Tape off loading and storing in an external location.
  • Tapes encryption.
  • Certified destruction of tapes after the retention period has finished.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel responsible for the backup/restore requires having a login with full administrative access on the server.
  • Cegeka must be allowed to install backup software on the server.
  • The platform in scope of the backup must be compatible with our backup software.
  • Restore operations always require a suitable target environment. Provisioning of such a target environment is not included in the service.
  • Cegeka advises to execute data and system restores on a regular basis to test backup and restore strategy and capabilities. These restore tests will be handled as normal restore requests, to be requested by <klant> via the RFC process or by setting up and approving a project letter. These tests will always be billed on a time and material basis (not included in the service fee).

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Cloud Backup

 

 

Management of the components of the backup infrastructure in the Cegeka datacenters.

 

R, A

Make data backups

 

R, A

R, A

R, A

C

C

0340_0010_0010_A_S.000 D2D - Client level back-up / image level backup - per 10 GB on backup medium

Archiving

Introduction

The archiving service provides CAS archiving capabilities:

  • To store large amounts of cloud native application data and/or infrequently accessed data safely and economically.
  • To store data on a geo redundant, scalable enterprise storage solution (geo-redundancy: Belgium (Hasselt) - The Netherlands (Geleen)
  • From which you can access data geo redundantly via:
    • RestFull API
    • S3 (fully compliant)
    • CIFS
  • That is HIPAA and SEC 17a-4 , which insures the Customer of data consistency at all times. European counter parts are Data Protection Directive 95/46/EC and GDPR
  • Which has no single point of failures and protects its data via erasure coding over multiple geographical locations.

The Shared Object Storage Service manages the components of the Object Storage platform infrastructure. These components are:

  • The object storage data serving nodes;
  • The object storage;
  • CIFS agents.

Service description

Standard services

  • CAS data archiving solution
  • Access via all listed access protocols
  • Data traffic (in/out)
  • Object creation
  • Object access/retrieval
  • Object deletion
  • Life cycle transitions
  • Custom metadata
  • Metadata search capabilities
  • Replicated data by default

Optional features

The following options can only be deployed after technical review:

  • Openstack/Swift
  • HDFS (Hadoop)

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

  • Performance: note that this service has a performance, which is similar to “internet-speed”. The storage is not intended as a fast storage for files and objects. Compare this to Amazon S3 and Glacier storage.
  • Not a drop in replacement for NAS: while the service can offer NAS functionality, it is not intended as a NAS replacement. The NAS access is only intended as an additional interface to the data.
  • NFS: Supported NAS functionality is NFSv3
  • CIFS: CIFS is based on a client to be installed on a server. The compatibilities matrix of the CIFS agent is leading for which systems are supported for being the CIFS front-end
  • S3 API: The system is not fully compliant with the S3 API
  • Search/Metadata: Metadata searches are possible. There is a limit on the number of metadata fields that can be used.

Part number

O.0350_0010_0010_A_S.000 CAS per 10 GB

O.0350_0010_0020_A_S.000 CAS per 10 GB Georeplicated

Hardware Datacenter (Lease)

Introduction

For certain services, Cegeka uses dedicated hardware mostly for license technical reasons or for specific workloads. The most common applications are: Systems with a workplace OS, Oracle systems, specific database clusters and Central workplace infrastructure.

Dedicated systems are also used for applications at office locations for distributing software and / or files (Branch office / Cloud distribution).

This service is based on a financial lease in which Cegeka owns the relevant systems (hardware) during the fixed term. After the run, <klant> has the opportunity to purchase the relevant systems (hardware).

This service is usually closed for a period of 36 or 60 months.

The delivered hardware in our solution are specified in Appendix 8, Client Hardware and Software.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

Administrative processing and registration in the CMDB.

Optional services

N/A

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

The Cegeka is responsible for the systems (assets) in the datacenters of Cegeka.

<klant> is responsible for the systems (assets) that are not on Cegeka locations. This implies that <klant> is responsible for damage to these systems (assets) that are outside the control of Cegeka. This damage is capped to the remaining instalments of the fixed term of the relevant system (Asset).

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<KLANT>

CEGEKA

HW Lease and support

 

 

Support on system in datacenters of Cegeka (under Cegeka contract)

I

R, A

Support on systems on sites of the <klant> (for example with a carepack)

R, A

I

O.0370_0050_0010_A_I.000 NAS (HPE) - StoreEasy 1860 NAS (15,28 TB) - 36 MND

Software

Introduction

Cegeka can provide serval licenses to <klant>, based on financial lease with a fixed period (This service is usually closed for a period of 36 or 60 months) and/or in a subscription model on a monthly basis.

License compliancy is always a responsibility of <klant>.

The delivered software in our solution are specified in Appendix 8, Client Hardware and Software.

FTP

Introduction

The Shared FTP service provides a platform to exchange files using the FTP(*), sFTP and FTPS protocol. Via a secured FTP portal (user management interface) access can be granted to the FTP server platform where file data can be loaded or downloaded.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka is responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the Shared FTP service infrastructure in the Cegeka datacenters;
  • Access to the FTP portal (user management interface);
  • Delivery of one admin user account to create and manage user accounts in the user management interface (web portal);
  • Capability of retrieving (exporting to .csv) and analyzing the FTP-log files. To be able to securely connect to the web interface, a client certificate will be provided;
  • Supported protocols:
        • FTP (*)
        • SFTP (Secure FTP – SSH)
        • FTPS (FTP – SSL)

(*) The plain-text FTP service is in legacy-only mode

Optional services (not included)

  • The data in the FTP shares is not backupped by default. If needed the FTP service can be added to the service ‘Backup’.

Conditions

  • The use of the service ‘Monitoring/Event Management’ is mandatory to be able to check if the FTP-share still has enough space
  • Access to the web portal only possible using the client certificate

Part number

O.0320_0080_0030_A_S.000 File-level – FTP per GB

SMS Gateway

Introduction

The SMS gateway service provides a platform to send SMS messages worldwide (e.g. one-time passwords, alerts ...) which originate from <klant> systems (soft- and hardware platforms) in the Cegeka datacenters.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka is responsible in providing the following Services:

  • The API (RESTful)to connect the systems to the SMS gateway and to connect to the SMS Cloud broker;
  • Basic reports on the sent SMS messages via http://notify.cegeka.com (please refer to the ‘Reporting’ service for more advanced reports, the pricing for these advanced reports are not included).
  • Hardware and software platform: SMS internal (BE) gateways, internal SMS antennas, SMS gateway appliances and SMS software;
  • Cloud provider for SMS messages.

Conditions

  • The API supported connection is RESTful.
  • This service is intended to send SMS messages coming from systems. This service is not intended for human usage (people sending SMS messages to one another).

Part number

O.0610_0050_0010_A_S.000 Token Messaging - SMS gateway subscription

O.0610_0050_0020_A_S.000 Token Messaging - SMS text messages

Security Services

Firewall Services

Introduction

The shared firewall service consists of four redundant firewall nodes, split up over the availability zones of Hasselt and Geleen. This configuration guarantees full redundancy. In a DRP-scenario, all new sessions will fail over automatically to the next standby node.

The platform is based on Checkpoint VSX technology. <klant> is provided with its own dedicated virtual firewall, allowing for multi gigabit performance and redundancy.

Each dedicated virtual firewall contains a <klant> specific rule base (in accordance with the Cegeka rule base convention).

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Controls access between separate networks on L4 of the OSI model;
  • Executes address translation between separate networks (NAT/Hiding);
  • Rules management via Standard Request;
  • Management of the following components:
    • Firewall nodes;
    • Firewall software;
    • Management station;
    • Management software.
  • Service Characteristics (Dedicated “Rulebase” Virtual firewall)

This type of firewall has the following features:

  • No limitation on the number of VLANs
  • External connections to/from the VLANs behind the firewall (e.g.: Leased Line, MPLS …) are allowed (e.g.: to connect the servers with an office environment)
  • Policy based site-to-site VPNs are possible
  • Custom feature/parameters are allowed (e.g. state table limits, state full inspection parameters, protocol parameters, QoS settings, client authentication, …)
  • Custom parameters are allowed (State table entries, IPS, TCP/UDP, IP … settings)
  • Firewall reporting (log reporting):
    • Statistics per VLAN interface
      • Dropped connections
      • Dropped source IP addresses
      • Dropped Per TCP / UDP ports
  • IPS functionality is included and based on the Cegeka policy.
    • The customer can opt for a customized IPS policy (non-standard request)
  • The private IP addresses used behind the firewall can be chosen freely by the customer

Optional services

  • Application control:
    • The ability to identify, allow, block or limit usage of known applications and widgets;
    • Applications are classified categories based on type, risk level … ;
    • Examples: YouTube, TeamViewer, Facebook.
  • URL filtering:
    • The ability to identify, allow, block or limit usage of known categories;
      • Custom categories or URL’s can be added;
    • Examples: High Risk, Social Networking.
  • Identity awareness:
    • Allows a security policy to be built using identities instead of (source) IPs;
    • AD groups can also be used within a security policy to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Anti-virus:
    • Identifies malware and malicious websites;
    • Stop incoming malicious files;
    • Prevents access to malware infested websites.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

R, A

I, C

I, C

R, A

I, C

Management dedicated platforms Firewall Security

Introduction

Firewall Security services is Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, security, administration and planning of all functions relating to dedicated firewall and other network related security components.

The Firewall Security service releases <klant> from the day-to-day management of the Firewall Security. Cegeka manages the Firewall on a remote (i.e. off-site) basis from our operation center, proactively fixing faults and keeping the service running. Cegeka also provide reports on the status of your firewall (and other network related security components) and informs <klant> about incidents that occur.

Service description

Standard features

The following standard features are included in this Service:

  • Device performance optimization, Maintain best practice performance optimization techniques:
    • drop optimization
    • state tables
    • throughput acceleration
    • connection-rate acceleration
    • worker processes/instances
    • interface optimization
    • interface buffering
    • traffic inspection
  • Log file management, Security Logs are collected and archived for at least 6 months. (The default log format used is ‘syslog’. Specialized tooling can be used to convert product specific log formats towards the standard syslog format.)
  • Rule base maintenance, Rule bases tend naturally toward disorder over time, and as the size of the ruleset grows, the performance of the security device starts to suffer. Recurrent rule base tuning (1x per year) consists out of:
    • Disable unused rules.
    • Remove unused objects.
    • Order rules (most used before least used).
    • Maintain naming convention on objects/rules/security zones…
    • Analyze potential security risks (ex: ‘Any’ in source, destination or service)
  • Security Management, Manage security policies against security best practices to provide protection for the information systems while still allowing the attainment of business objective and preserving the integrity, availability and confidentiality of the information resources.
  • Signature or anomaly based detection, Network Intrusion Detection/Prevention Systems (IDS/IPS) monitor computer network traffic and attempt to identify, alert, and present all anomalous activity to the protected hosts. Traditionally, IPS and IDS systems depend on signatures to operate. Because of this, it is extremely important to tune the IPS/IDS device accordingly and to develop policies and procedures to continuously update the signatures. By default, devices will be configured to download and implement signature updates automatically. On request specific procedures can be arranged.
  • Availability monitoring of the firewall and other security network related security components.
  • Incident Management, second level support, and the Request and Change Management concerning configuration changes: add, change, disposal (charged in T&M – standard change list):
    • Call intake / service desk activities (1st line) are at <klant> site or Cegeka.
    • Incident and problem management includes the creation and follow up of tickets at vendors / constructors if applicable. This implies that a valid maintenance contract (break and fix) is available.
  • Also included is asset management. All Configuration Items are stored in the Cegeka CMDB. The firewall and other network related security components devices and functionality is monitored proactively by a central monitoring system in the Cegeka datacenter in terms of performance, errors and capacity. In case of problems or abnormal circumstances, the systems will be supported remotely.

Scope

The scope of the Firewall Security service covers the management of all active and manageable physical firewalls and other network related security components.

Cegeka’s service desk Services are mandatory to deliver this Remote LAN Management Service.

Optional feature to this service

The following feature(s) are considered to be optional features to this service:

  • Hardware maintenance contracts
  • Hardware refresh and the design aspects of the Firewall/Security setup.

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the Firewall/Security device.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access to the Firewall/Security device.

Dependencies

Mandatory: “Service Desk” (2nd line)

Location

The service can be delivered for all Client locations and the Cegeka datacenters.

Ownership

The equipment is being owned by <klant> (or in a lease construction)

Service reporting

  • Reports as part of the monthly service review: availability in the general SLA report.

Responsibility

O.0390_0020_0020_A_S.000 Management dedicated platforms -Firewall Security

Anti-virus

Introduction

This Service ensures the security of the operating system against malware, viruses and rootkits. It also protects the managed Windows Workplace service from threats at the network level.

The anti-virus agent handles scanning of local file systems during read and write operations. The agents are installed on the workplace and the Windows backend servers. The centrally managed anti-virus solution automatically deploys updates to each agent and provides an overview over the agent status and events.

The anti-virus service manages the components of the anti-virus infrastructure in the Cegeka datacenters. By enabling this solution, the anti-virus clients, even when connected via internet, will be updated by the latest anti-virus definitions from the centrally managed solution.

The update of the agent will follow the existing “Windows update patching mechanism” and will go through the Standard Change process with a fixed deploy governance.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • The hardware of the anti-virus platform;
  • The anti-virus software and licenses on device level;
  • Management of the anti-virus solution;
  • The definitions incorporated in the anti-virus software;
  • The follow up in case a device is infected with a virus. Every alert will be registered at the service desk at appropriate priority level;
  • The anti-virus IDS operates on every machine in an enterprise where the anti-virus agent is installed by analysing network packets targeted at the network node and comparing them with signature database entries. An IDS helps identify attacks and probes by monitoring traffic for attack signatures that represent hostile activity.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

WAN/LAN Encryption

Introduction

Cegeka delivers (with subcontractor Arcadiz) LAN/WAN encryption on layer 2 level based on AES256 encryption.

Service description

Standard services

  • Housing of the Encryption equipment in the Cegeka Datacenters.
  • Within the Arcadiz service:
    • Software Release Support (updates and upgrades)
    • Monitoring the encryption equipment 24x7
    • Automated NOC for Fault & Event Detection
    • Back office services to reproduce faults
    • Preventive Maintenance
    • Advanced Product Replacement (spare parts management)

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

Conditioned space with rack space on the customer side (near the demarcation points), access for installation and in the event of HW failures.

O.0455_0010_0010_M_E.000 WAN (Internal Fiber) Encryption

Vulnerability scanning

This chapter is considered to be a draft outline and will be discussed in a workshop if <klant> decides to implement this solution.

Introduction

Cegeka uses an automated SaaS security scanner that enables <klant> to diagnose external network vulnerabilities and secure sensitive data.

The scanner examines network perimeters, identifies vulnerabilities and inventories actionable remedies, effectively preventing cyber criminals from penetrating <klant>’s network. By alerting you immediately upon detection of new threats, <klant> remains in control and can maintain business continuity.

Availability

  • The solution is a SaaS solution and therefore availability is covered by the SaaS vendor.

The local appliance is a black box hosted on the virtual platform and thus can take advantage of VMware HA. This is a single node, but can be extend with a second node to provide a clustered setup.

Scalability

  • The solution fit internal and external networks of all sizes and can integrate with different appliances to increase scalability and span vast networks.

Performance

  • The solution is a SaaS solution and therefore performance scales towards the needs. No extra costs are involved.
  • The local appliance has its limitations but for the current scope it suffices.

Manageability

  • The solution uses role-based user administration
  • two-factor authentication log-in is possible, but not provided in the standard offering
  • AD integration is possible

Backup & Recovery

  • Backup of the SaaS solution is provided by the vendor
  • The local appliance is a black box and does not contain intelligence and is therefore not placed in backup.

Disaster Recovery

  • The solution is a SaaS solution and therefore Disaster Recovery is covered by the SaaS vendor.

Service description

This service can be employed in two different setups:

External vulnerability scanning:

  • Scan originating from the internet to scan public IP addresses;
  • Gives an indication of the overall security of an environment
    (taking into account active security measures on all layers).

Internal vulnerability scanning:

  • Scan originating from within the private network to scan private IP addresses;
  • Uses a virtual appliance to scan systems directly;
  • Gives an indication of the security of one specific component (without taking the protection offered by other layers into account).

Responsibilities

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Asset inventory: CMDB information verification/update/cleaning;
  • Vulnerability Management: Technical vulnerability assessment of network/infrastructure;
  • Policy Compliance: Evaluate and document compliance with our defined policies in detailed reports;

O.0410_0010_0010_A_S.000 Price per scan (per IP or per URL) external
