O.0410_0010_0030_M_TM.000 Periodic scanning service support

Web Application Firewall

Introduction

Cegeka provides customer with a reverse proxy service, which also acts as an application firewall, which protects against various application attacks, including

  • AJAX/JSON web threats;
  • Layer 7 DoS and DDoS;
  • Brute force;
  • Cross-site scripting (XSS);
  • Cross Site Request Forgery;
  • SQL injection;
  • Sensitive information leakage;
  • Session high jacking;
  • Buffer overflows;
  • Cookie manipulation;
  • Various encoding attacks;
  • Broken access control;
  • Forceful browsing;
  • Hidden fields manipulation;
  • Request smuggling;
  • XML bombs/DoS;

The reverse proxy service also acts as a SSL/TLS termination proxy to handle incoming SSL/TLS connections, decrypting the traffic and passing the unencrypted requests to the application server.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the application firewall and reverse proxy;
  • Generation of alerts in case of a security incident.

Optional services

This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0440_0010_0010_A_S.000 Web application firewall - per VIP/tcp port combination (F5 ASM)

Secure mail

Introduction

For email servers that are hosted within the Cegeka datacenter, Cegeka can offer anti-spam and anti-virus measures to protect incoming and outgoing email. The service is based on Cisco IronPort

SPAM filtering is the process of determining whether a given mail is unsolicited and reacting accordingly by deleting, quarantining, tagging this mail. Virus scanning is running the files, attached to a mail through a virus scanner, and blocking infected mails, to prevent viruses from entering the network. Virus scanning uses standard available anti-virus software, which is embedded in the email security gateway.

SPAM filtering uses a number of different techniques to determine whether mails are legitimate.

  1. Reputation filtering: each sending mail server’s ip address is given a reputation based on historical performance: the number of mails through this ip address, the amount of SPAM coming from this address, the number of blacklists this address is listed in, etc. This results in a number, the reputation score. Based on this score, different actions can be taken for mails (good reputation makes the mails pass, bad reputation blocks the mail. The Ironport Email Security appliances use the reputation service from Senderbase.org

b) Pattern matching: the appliances have an internal database of known words, phrases, that occur in SPAM messages. All mail is checked against this database, to determine whether they are SPAM or not.

The customer can choose to use the standard policy configuration that Cegeka manages or can request to have a dedicated and specific policy to match (more or less stringent) requirements a customer has.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the platform and the anti-virus and anti-spam definitions
  • Management of the standard and specific policies

Optional services

Advanced Malware Protection

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

  • The service may not be used for sending bulk (commercial) email. If a customer sends (or receives) bulk mailings that jeopardize the mail flow through the e-mail security gateway, the mail traffic of this customer will be throttled.
  • The customer must configure its servers or systems to adhere to SMTP guidelines and protocols when using the Cegeka service. When the e-mail traffic of a customer impedes the distribution of the e-mail traffic over all the available platform nodes, this customer’s e-mail traffic will be throttled. If Cegeka is responsible for managing the email server(s) of a customers, these demands are met by default.

The throttling is temporarily and only until the data flow traffic normalizes.

Part number

O.0450_0010_0010_A_S.000 Secure mail gateway (IronPort) - including 5000 emails (in and out)

O.0450_0010_0020_A_S.000 Secure mail gateway (IronPort) - additional emails (per 100 in and out)

Network Services

Datacenter LAN Services

Introduction

The datacenter LAN in the network for connecting and interconnecting the infrastructure.

Within the datacenter LAN, there are 2 sub layers:

  • Collapsed core layer
  • Access layer

The core layer is used for:

  • Interconnecting the access layer
  • Interconnecting datacenters

All equipment to the core is connected redundantly.

The access layer is used for:

  • Networking equipment (e.g. firewall services, routers, remote access, backup switches)
  • Endpoints, such as servers, blade chassis
  • Connections to the access layer can be through a layer 2 switched port or through a layer 3 routed port.

Cegeka’s datacenter LAN design consists of a redundant Cisco Nexus 7000 series core switches.

The same type of setup is deployed in the access layer. The setup consists of 2x 5000 series Cisco Nexus switches connected to 2000 series Cisco Nexus fabric extenders (FEX). Servers in the datacenter are always redundantly connected to two different fabric extenders. These fabric extenders are connected to different Nexus 5000 parent switches.

The access switches are LACP port-channels. This ensures redundancy via the aggregation of two physical cables into one logical connection. Blade switches are configured with link state groups. In case of an uplink failure in the core switches, the link status is propagated to the server connections in the chassis.

On the server side, all network interface cards are redundantly patched on separate access switches.

An endpoint that requires 1 Gbps or less is connected to a Nexus 2000 switch. It is absolutely recommended to dual home the server by connecting it to the network by at least 2 cables. The network must be configured in an active/passive manner.

An endpoint that requires 10 Gbps or more is connected to a Nexus 5000 switch. In every other aspect, it’s the same as an endpoint that is connected at 1 Gbps. As a standard, the server shall be connected to the network by at least 2 cables. The network shall be configured in an active/passive manner.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the components of datacenter LAN infrastructure in the Cegeka datacenters. Components managed:
    • Core switches;
    • Access switches;
    • Cabling and termination equipment (e.g. GBICs).
  • Delivery of the platform in the Cegeka datacenters to offer Ethernet on a physical carrier:
    • Connect servers to the central switching infrastructure
    • Divide networks in segments (VLANs)
    • Group multiple network ports in a link aggregate (trunking)
  • Delivery of the following speeds, depending on server requirements:
    • 1 Gbps/100Mbps
    • 10 Gbps

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

The equipment must contain industry standard GBIC’s or Ethernet ports to be connected to the datacenter LAN. For servers that are sized by Cegeka, this is guaranteed.

Management dedicated platforms LAN

Introduction

Managed LAN services is Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, administration and planning of all functions relating to infrastructure and components of local LAN network.

The Managed LAN service releases <klant> from the day-to-day management of the LAN infrastructure. Cegeka manages the LAN network on a remote (i.e. off-site) basis from our operation center, proactively fixing faults and keeping the service running. Cegeka also provides reports on the status of your network and informs <klant> about incidents that occur.

Service description

Standard features

The following standard features are included in this Service:

  • Availability monitoring of the switches.
  • Incident Management, second level support, and the Request and Change Management concerning configuration changes: add, change, disposal (charged in T&M – standard change list):
    • Call intake / service desk activities (1st line) are at <klant> site or Cegeka.
    • Incident and problem management includes the creation and follow up of tickets at vendors / constructors if applicable. This implies that a valid maintenance contract (break and fix) is available.
  • Also included is asset management. All Configuration Items are stored in the Cegeka CMDB. The LAN devices and functionality is monitored proactively by a central monitoring system in the Cegeka datacenter in terms of performance, errors and capacity. In case of problems or abnormal circumstances, the systems will be supported remotely.

Scope

The scope of the Managed LAN service covers the management of all active and manageable physical LAN components.

The demarcation line is the starting point of the WAN infrastructure of the WAN service provider. The WAN connectivity (incl. routers) and management of the servers and desktops is not part of this service. These functions can be delivered via other Cegeka services.

Cegeka’s service desk Services are mandatory to deliver this Remote LAN Management Service.

Certain LAN Switches have network modules or network management functionality (e.g. load balancing, firewall, blade server, virtual chassis, etc.). The management of such functionality is not included in the LAN Service.

Optional feature to this service

The following feature(s) are considered to be optional features to this service:

  • Hardware maintenance contracts
  • Hardware refresh and the design aspects of the LAN setup.

Conditions

  • All switches must be manageable and valid maintenance contracts (break and fix) are available.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the switches.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on switches.

Dependencies

Mandatory: “Service Desk” (2nd line)

Location

The service can be delivered for all Client locations and the Cegeka datacenters.

Ownership

The equipment is being owned by <klant>

Service reporting

  • Reports as part of the monthly service review: availability in the general SLA report.
  • Reports available upon request: Overview of switches managed by this service.

O.0460_0050_0020_A_S.000 LAN switch Advanced

Site-to-Site VPN Services

Introduction

The Cegeka offering includes Site-to-Site connectivity for customers or third parties towards the customer networks in the datacenter based on IPSec connections over the Internet (or when required, over WAN connections). Depending on the type of VPN tunnel and the number of tunnels required, Cegeka will either host the tunnel end-point on the customer dedicated virtual firewall instance or on separate VPN end-point hardware.

Both Policy Based and Route Based VPNs are supported. Note that with route based VPNs, it is possible to create high-available VPN connections (by means of secondary VPN connections that can be automatically failed over to in case of primary connection failure) and also to use these as backup to a primary (routed) WAN connection.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the VPN end-point hardware in the Cegeka datacenters
  • Configuration and maintenance of the Cegeka side of the VPN tunnel

Optional services

N/A

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0480_0010_0010_A_S.000 VPN Monthly cost for site-to-site VPN - Policy-based policy based

O.0480_0010_0020_A_S.000 VPN Monthly cost for site-to-site VPN - Route-based route based

Wireless LAN (AccesPoint)

Introduction

Managed Wireless LAN (AccesPoint) services is Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, administration and planning of all functions relating to infrastructure and components of Wireless LAN.

Service description

Standard features

The following standard features are included in this Service:

  • Availability monitoring of the access points, based on alerts generated by a AccesPoint or WLAN controller;
  • Incident Management:
    • Second level support;
    • Call intake / service desk activities (1st line) at <klant> or Cegeka;
    • Incident and problem management includes the creation and follow up of tickets at vendors / constructors if applicable. This implies that a valid maintenance contract (break and fix) is available.
  • Request and Change Management concerning configuration changes: add, change, disposal (charged in T&M – standard change list);
  • Patch management of the controllers and access points is provided on a pro-active level. The patching is executed according to the Change Management Process;
  • Also included is asset management. All Configuration Items are stored in the CMDB. The WLAN devices and functionality is monitored proactively by a central monitoring system.

Optional feature to this service

The following feature(s) are considered to be optional features to this service:

  • Hardware maintenance contracts and the IMACD services (Installation, Move, Add, Change, and Disposal).
  • Hardware refresh and the design aspects of the WLAN setup.
  • Be able to encrypt the connection;
  • Have the possibility of authentication based on RADIUS;
  • Offer "guest access" through a personalized captive portal;
  • Use a management interface for issuing temporary accounts for 'guest access' on the basis of:
    • User id/password;
    • MAC address;
  • Be able to support QoS on the wireless network to enable wireless phone usage over the wireless network;
  • Be able to use Remote Access Points (RAPs);

Conditions

  • The service Wireless LAN manages the components of the Wireless LAN platform infrastructure. These components are:
    • Wireless controllers
    • Wireless access points, without physical cabling
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access and network access on the wireless controller (if available) and access points.

Dependencies

Mandatory: “Service Desk” (2nd line).

Location

The service can be delivered for all Client locations and the Cegeka datacenters.

Ownership

The equipment is being owned by the Client.

Service reporting

  • Reports as part of the monthly service review: availability in the SLA report.
  • Reports available upon request (T&M) and if supported by the configured tooling:
    • Overview of access points and wireless controllers managed by this Service.
    • Traffic and throughput/ dashboards on latency.

Responsibilities

O.0460_0050_0050_A_S.000 WLAN access point

Datacenter Interconnect

Introduction

The Cegeka datacenter connectivity service interconnects the datacenters. The Service is built using redundant Dense Wavelength Division Multiplexing (DWDM) devices. Eight Ethernet connections are configured on these devices. The interconnect lines are divided and managed by two separate Telco-providers ensuring the highest levels of redundancy. The four DWDM paths have a 100% geographical separated track. These paths have no components in common. The Ethernet and fiber channel connections have their own dedicated fibers. Both LAN and SAN have a dedicated bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In view of maximum redundancy and availability, all eight links are joined together into a multi-chassis Ethernet channel. In case of failure on one or more connections, the data traffic will be switched over immediately to one of the remaining connections.

The latency between the two datacenters is low. A roundtrip time of 2 to 4ms on the Ethernet connection and a maximum of 2ms on the fiber channel.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Replication of fiber channel and Ethernet (LAN extension).
  • Cegeka continuously monitors the used bandwidth and performs capacity trending to avoid future possible bottlenecks.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • One stream can generate maximal 10 Gbps.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

Datacenter interconnect

Monitor interconnect

R, A

Capacity management

R, A

Manage encryption keys

R, A

Service Part number(s)

O.0470_0010_0100_M_S.000 Use of the Shared LAN Datacenter Interconnect (Hasselt – Geleen)

Loadbalancer Services

Introduction

The load balancing is performed based on Cegeka’s load balancing managed service. The setup consists of redundant F5 LTM controllers. The load balancing is capable of load balancing internal and external applications.

Service description

Standard features

The load balancing service provides a platform to load balance applications, which are installed on servers in or outside the Cegeka datacenters.

The Service consists of:

  • The use of the load balancing platform;
  • The use of the management portal to:
    • Activate or deactivate systems which are load balanced;
    • Consult load balancing statistics.

The load balancing platform is not limited to http/https based applications. Each application, which uses a particular TCP or UDP port can be load balanced. The application needs to be load balancing aware.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

To be able to use load balancing, an active production connection with one of the Cegeka datacenters needs to be in place.

Responsibilities

<klant>

CEGEKA

Recommend load balancing service best practices

C

R, A

Add/change/remove load balancing configuration

C

R, A

Service Part number(s)

O.0490_0010_0010_A_S.000 Load balancing - per VIP/tcp port combination (F5 LTM)

Management dedicated platforms Load Balancer

Introduction

Managed dedicated Load Balancer services is Cegeka managed service which consist of the monitoring, management, maintenance, supervision, administration and planning of all functions relating to the infrastructure and components of the load balancer environment,

Cegeka manages the loadbalancer components on a remote (i.e. off-site) basis from our operation center, proactively fixing faults and keeping the service running. Cegeka also provide reports on the status of the loadbalancer infrastructure and informs <klant> about incidents that occur.

Service description

Standard features

The load balancing service provides manages the platform installed on servers or appliances in or outside the Cegeka datacenters.

The following standard features are included in this Service:

  • Availability monitoring of the load balancers
  • Incident Management, second level support, and the Request and Change Management concerning configuration changes: add, change, disposal (charged in T&M – standard change list):
    • Call intake / service desk activities (1st line) are at <klant> site or Cegeka.
    • Incident and problem management includes the creation and follow up of tickets at vendors / constructors if applicable. This implies that a valid maintenance contract (break and fix) is available.
  • Also included is asset management. All Configuration Items are stored in the Cegeka CMDB. The load balancer devices and functionality is monitored proactively by a central monitoring system in the Cegeka datacenter in terms of performance, errors and capacity. In case of problems or abnormal circumstances, the systems will be supported remotely.

Optional services

The following feature(s) are considered to be optional features to this service:

  • Hardware maintenance contracts
  • Hardware refresh and the design aspects of the load balancer setup

Out of scope

All <klant> initiated changes (requests) on the standard security policy are not included in this service

Conditions

  • All load balancers components must be manageable and valid maintenance contracts (break and fix) are available.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have network access to the load balancers.
  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with full administrative access on the load balancers.


Part number
O.0490_0020_0020_A_S.000 Loadbalancer Services - Management dedicated platforms (Advanced)

Internet access

Introduction

Internet connectivity is a standard managed service of Cegeka. The set-up of the internet connection is fully redundant on all layers.

The Shared Internet connectivity service provides a platform to:

  • Get internet access from the Cegeka datacenters (on IP-level);
  • Use NTP for time synchronization (URL: ntp.cegeka.be);
  • Management of the Cegeka public IP ranges;
  • Management of provider independent public IP-ranges that are transferred to Cegeka:
    • Client is member of RIPE NCC and will maintain its duties as a “LIR” (local internet registry). Cegeka will support Client with the remaining activities for the management of the public IP- addresses (e.g. relation with Cegeka’s ISP, AS (Autonomous System) number, etc.).

The service is protected against DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service).

Service description

The shared managed Internet connectivity service has no limitation on bandwidth used by <klant>’s, apart from the total available physical capacity. The usage is monitored and evaluated. The internet access consists of multiple Gbps internet connections. As soon as there is a not enough bandwidth available for the shared service, the bandwidth is upgraded.

The consumed bandwidth of <klant> will be monitored and invoiced based on the PxQ model where only the 95th percentile is taken into account on monthly basis. The bandwidth usage during a month is measured each 5 minutes. All these samples are ordered and the top 5 percent of measurements is discarded (to avoid charging for brief peak usage). The mark that is then the highest is used as the reference for the Internet usage charge. By discarding the top 5% there is on average about 30 to 36 hours a month of peak/burst usage possible without being charged for that.

Cegeka does not cap the bandwidth, to avoid incidents with respect to bandwidth shortage. Cegeka has more than sufficient bandwidth available to sustain the load of <klant>. A strict capacity management process is in place consisting of alerting on above 60% usage of our Internet bandwidth and additionally manually checked by an engineer each week. When we hit the 60% mark, an upgrade of capacity will implemented, to ensure sufficient capacity for the <klant>.

<klant> can bring its own Public IP addresses to the Cegeka cloud. As part of the migration the public IP addresses will be made available and announced that this IP address range is now available in the Cegeka datacenter.

Cegeka can provide public IP addresses to <klant>. This is an option in case the public IP addresses are owned by the current provider and cannot be migrated to the Cegeka datacenter.

Cegeka uses Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) for the internet access with own public IP addresses which can easily move from one to another Internet provider. The Cegeka set-up consists of 2x2 redundant BGP routers to separate ISP providers.

The Cegeka datacenters have their own AS (Autonomous System) number. In order to have an AS-number, a service provider needs to comply with many requirements. One of the requirements is that the connections are dual-homed with two independent internet providers.

Standard features

The Internet connectivity service manages and administrates the components of the Internet connectivity service infrastructure in the Cegeka datacenters. These components are:

  • BGP routers
  • IP uplinks to the telecom providers
  • DDOS Protectors and reporting
  • The traffic scrubbing street
  • Security Monitoring Service integration
  • The syslog feed will be sent to Client for retention requirements

The Cegeka Shared Internet connection has a dual layer protection against DDoS attacks. In the Cegeka datacenters there are inline anti-DDoS protection devices that can protect against non-volumetric DDoS attacks (e.g. attacks based on protocol level, like ACK attacks) and that block these attacks typically within 18 seconds or less. For volumetric attacks, Cegeka uses a scrubbing street. As this requires a BGP rerouting towards the scrubbing street, the time for this to take effect is slightly higher. Cegeka can re-route certain blocks of her IP addresses and only reroutes blocks that are under attack. The rerouted attack traffic is separated from the rerouted valid traffic by the scrubbing street and only the valid traffic is then provided to Cegeka. Due to the shared nature of the Internet connection, other customers under attack may influence <klant> and vice versa. In practice this seldom leads to issues and due to the use of the scrubbing street any impact is only short-lived. For reference, in the past two years Cegeka only activated the scrubbing street pro-actively (before any actual DDoS attack, based on market information and threat intel)

When <klant> is under DDoS attack for more than 15 minutes, the scrubbing street will be activated.

In case of security incidents, Client will be notified according to the agreed incident management procedures.

Optional features

Cegeka can provide Client with public IP addresses. This is an option in case the public IP addresses are owned by the current provider and cannot be migrated to the Cegeka datacentre.

Conditions:

Cegeka reserves the right to remove specific systems from the shared internet connection (“black holing”) in case such systems are targeted by a DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) attack or any other form of attack that may have a negative impact on the availability and quality of service of the shared internet service.

WAN Services

Introduction

Cegeka delivers (with subcontractor Eurofiber) the connectivity to our datacenters based on layer 2. Ethernet VPN connects your head office and the datacenters securely and flexibly via a customized private network. (after the transition only routed traffic on layer 3 is allowed).

Service description

Standard services

The following standard features are included in the Eurofiber service:

  • Availability monitoring of the switches.
  • Incident Management and the Request and Change Management concerning configuration changes
  • Management of the E-LAN multi-point EVC configuration between three or more locations
  • Manage the configuration as a LAN configuration as an Ethernet Virtual Private LAN (EPP-LAN).
  • Premium - 99.9% guarantee on the bandwidth and minimum delay and jitter on your connections.

Scope

The scope of the Managed WAN service covers the management of all active and manageable components in the Eurofiber network.

The demarcation For the Ethernet VPN service is the ODF placed by Eurofiber at the building entrance. The service is delivered as standard via a CPE (demarcation point) that is placed in the customer cabinet, with a maximum of 25 meters after building entry. Eurofiber is responsible for the cabling between the ODF and this CPE.

Optional services

This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

At Cegeka DC site this service requires 10GB access ports and in the running phase only layer 3 traffic is allowed.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<klant>

CEGEKA

General Roles and Responsibilities

Change management

C

AR

Service Part number(s)

O. O.0510_0020_0010_M_S.000 WAN link Eurofiber ELAN The Rock AMS ‘Dual Homed’ to DC Hasselt and Geleen (4x 1GB premium)

Cloud Connect Services

Introduction

Cegeka delivers connectivity to almost all public cloud services. This Cegeka service is based on dedicated network connections over the Cegeka shared ExpressRoutes to the Interxion Cloud Connect

Interxion Cloud Connect is a layer 2 software defined network (SDN) that connects Interxion's datacenters across 11 European countries. It creates virtual point to point circuits from Cloud Connect ports to the Cegeka network. To maintain the security of the Cloud Connect platform, connection is only made over secure and private layer 2 networking.

Resilience is built in to the Cloud Connect service architecture. We use dual platforms for redundancy, installed in separate physical datacenters, network services diverse fiber feeds.

Service description

Standard services

  • The Cegeka Cloud Connect Services provides a redundant dedicated network connection to the Interxion Cloud Connect Service.
  • Supported Cloud Connections:
  • Microsoft ExpressRoute
  • AWS Direct Connect Google
  • Google Cloud Interconnect
  • Microsoft Azure Private Peering is default for Microsoft ExpressRoute

Scope

The Cloud Connect Service manages the components in the Cegeka Private Cloud and the redundant Connection between Cegeka WAN and Interxion.

Optional services

  • Optional Supported Cloud Connections:
  • Oracle Fast Connect
  • City Cloud Connect
  • IBM Direct Link
  • Tencent Direct Connect
  • Alibaba Cloud
  • Salesforce Express Connect
  • Microsoft Azure (former public) peering

Out of scope

The Public Cloud subscription is not part of this service.

Outages of the ExpressRoute service of Microsoft, caused by Microsoft, are out of scope of Cegeka.

Activities performance in the cloud tenant of the customer.

Conditions

Prerequisite: customer, Cegeka or 3th party needs to set up a Supported Cloud Connection (for example an ExpressRoute circuit) in the public cloud tenant of customer and provide service key in advance to Cegeka in order to successfully provision the service.

SSL Client-to-Site VPN Services

Introduction

The Cegeka offering includes remote access service based on SSL VPN. SSL VPN is the technological successor of IPSec as the standard for secure remote access to corporate applications. The remote access service provides a platform for the creation of client-to-site VPN tunnels and site-to-site VPN tunnels. The endpoint of the tunnel is customer’s network in the Cegeka datacenter.

7.3.2 Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Remote access is enabled through the BIG-IP Edge Client, a standalone or web-delivered client, on Mac, iPhone, iPad, Windows, Linux, and Android devices.
  • Authentication with username and password
  • Authentication with certificates
  • Split tunnelling or forced tunnelling
  • Granular access on IP-level

Optional services

Two-factor authentication

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part Number

O.0480_0010_0030_A_S.000 VPN Monthly cost for client-to-site VPN

Datacenter Services

Housing Services

Introduction

This Service offers a dedicated location for the installation of hardware in the Cegeka datacenters. In many cases this service is used for network equipment or is subject to other services used for specific applications, often license or workload driven (examples are: SQL clusters, workplace test systems and Citrix / VDI environments).

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • The physical location for the Housing Infrastructure platform.
  • Access control and security of the housing infrastructure platform.
  • Climate control (heating, ventilation and air conditioning) of the housing infrastructure platform.
  • Electrical installation of the housing infrastructure platform (electric distribution, generator, UPS system).
  • Fire detection and extinguishers of the Housing infrastructure platform.

There are 3 availability zones (AZ): AZ1 and AZ2 in datacenter Hasselt, AZ3 in datacenter Geleen.

Optional services

Cegeka offers the possibility to host non-managed devices:

  • Housing in a standard cage and billed per logical rack unit.
  • Housing in a ‘green’ cage and billed per logical rack unit and electrical usage.

Out-of-scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • The equipment must be industry standard 19” compatible.

Microsoft Azure Base Service

Introduction

The base service includes activities provided by Cegeka to manage continuously the surrounding platform components consumed by application workloads, like monitoring, authentication & authorization, compliance, cost management & billing. The base service also includes the management of some platform resources like VNets, monitoring and global backbone incidents.

By default, the management services related to the base service are included in a single fee, accounted per tenant, independently from the amount of subscriptions or resources.

All base services activities include reporting. Reporting is based on a standard template and is delivered monthly.

Automation

Cegeka leverages IaC (Infrastructure as Code) not only to deploy resources but also to keep them compliant with their configuration template during their lifecycle. IaC does not only increase the efficiency of initial resource deployment but also improves later configuration updates. Cegeka conducts configuration changes by changing the configuration template instead of changing directly the resource.

This approach provides multiple benefits: configuration templates are versioned so changes can be later audited, configuration templates also provide a global view on the entire configuration providing better insight about potential change impact.

Deployments are done according to best CI/CD practices and aligned with your processes.

Some of the different automation activities that Cegeka delivers are listed below.

  • Automation account
    • Create & manage Azure automation account
  • VM patching
    • Automate Azure VM patching based on Azure update management
    • Manage Update Management components
      • Log Analytics agent for Windows or Linux
      • PowerShell Desired State Configuration (DSC) for Linux
      • Automation Hybrid Runbook Worker
      • Microsoft Update or Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) for Windows machines
    • Check assessment results
    • Manage update schedule
    • Check update results
    • Reporting
  • Configuration & compliance management
    • Configuration compliance is automated => cfr compliance paragraph
  • Storage lifecycle
    • Automation of blob storage tiering based on lifecycle policies
  • Alerting
    • Enable & manage alerts automatically triggered in different circumstances (cfr monitoring chapter)
  • Automated deployment: templates/blueprints
    • The deployment of every resource is done based on IaC (Infrastructure as Code) using templates & blueprints
    • These templates & blueprints are securely (RBAC) and consistently (versioning) managed
  • Custom automation

Cegeka can deploy many other custom automation processes based on specific needs

RBAC

RBAC (Role Based Access Control) is the name given to the process regulating the permissions granted to users with regards to Azure resources. These permissions define the actions that a user is allowed to perform (ex: read only, configuration modification, switch on/off,…) on Azure resources. These permissions are resource specific so a user can have reader rights on a set of resource, owner rights on other resources and no right on the rest.

The RBAC management activity consists in continuously reviewing the state of these permissions, providing recommendations and modifying these permissions upon request.

Services

  • Review permissions configurations
    • RBAC roles aligned with least privilege principle (reader/contributor/owner)
    • RBAC scopes (resource-scoped Vs global rights)
    • MFA on privileged accounts (or all accounts if desired)
  • Review activity
    • Risky users & sign-ins (Identity Protection)
  • Report and provide recommendations

Remarks

  • Recurring activities based on AAD PIM feature (access reviews, identity protection) requiring AAD Premium P2 licenses
  • Custom pricing available on-demand in other cases

Governance and organization

Over time, as consumption scales, Azure resources tend to proliferate and could become hardly manageable without appropriate governance and organization.

The Cegeka resource/subscription management service ensures that Azure resources remain organized based on a pre-defined structure, for instance based on use case, delegation model or customer internal organization. This service also simplifies other daily operations by keeping resources conform with a predefined tagging and naming convention. Finally it ensures further scalability by checking that sufficient room remains compared to Azure limits and initiating pro-active actions when limits are getting close.

Services

  • Enforce compliancy of:
    • Azure regions and zones use (compliancy with predefined choices)
    • Azure tags
    • Naming convention
    • Resource SKU’s
  • Provide recommendations

Security best practices

The base service includes the management of globally applicable security mechanisms based on global tools (individual security components or configurations are covered separately). Essentially the service focusses on managing security tools like Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Azure DDoS and Azure advisor as well as ensuring that Cegeka and Microsoft security best practices are applied.

Services

  • Review information and alerts provided by
    • Microsoft Defender for Cloud free tier
    • Azure Advisor security recommendations
  • Review compliance with Cegeka security best practices
  • Provide recommendations

Networking

The VNet service includes the management of Azure networking resources providing basic connectivity inside Azure. Components providing advanced functionalities (LB, Application gateway,…) or connectivity outside of Azure are covered separately.

Covered components

  • Virtual Networks (including hub & spoke topologies)
  • Subnets
  • IP plan
  • NSGs

Services

  • Ensure that VNet components remain aligned with Cegeka best practices
  • Ensure that the IP plan remains well-structured and provides enough addressing space
  • Cleanup orphaned routing tables
  • Cleanup orphaned spokes
  • Cleanup orphaned NSGs
  • Troubleshoot NSGs
  • Troubleshoot routing tables
  • Troubleshoot peerings

Service Health

Service health provides a personalized view of the health of the Azure services and regions you're using. This is the best place to look for service impacting communications about outages, planned maintenance activities, and other health advisories because the authenticated Service Health experience knows which services and resources you currently use.

The management and resolution of backbone level incidents is included in the base service package. Backbone incidents include incidents not related to an individual resource like zonal/regional unavailability.

Billing

Cegeka provides billing services for the Azure consumption of CSP customers. This service is included inside the base service package. This doesn’t mean that the base service price includes Azure charges but that these Azure charges can be billed by Cegeka, jointly with other Cegeka services.

Monitoring

An Azure environment can be monitored by different tools but Cegeka by default uses Azure Monitor due to its native and deep integration with other Azure services.

Azure monitor provide an extensive set of functionalities allowing to gain visibility on the health and historical behaviour of Azure resources, including:

  • Health and status visibility
  • Log retention and analysis
  • Alerts

The activities included in this service being influenced by the amount and diversity of Azure resources, its price is included inside individual components.

This service includes the integration into the Cegeka Monitoring platform. This is needed to make sure alerts are triggered and the operational support people are informed immediately.

Services

  • Maintain alerts (otimize, repair…)
  • Respond to Service Health
  • Use Activity Logs for insights in resource-level events

Integrations

This service includes the integration into the Cegeka support platforms. This is needed to support and manage the environments with our engineers and architects, in an efficient and secure way.

You also get access to our incident management portal to allow incident creation and follow-up on support tickets.

Compliance

Azure offers a lot of new possibilities. The breed of possibilities may introduce a challenge to keep resources compliant without reducing choice freedom.

Cegeka helps solving this issue by decoupling compliancy from design. The compliancy service consists in managing a predefined set of compliancy rules and to leverage Azure policy to continuously monitor the compliancy state of deployed resources with the predefined rules.

Services

  • Review Azure resource hierarchy compliancy
    • Management groups and subscriptions
  • Review compliancy CIS LVL1, CIS LVL2 policies
  • Review Security Posture for Azure
  • Review automated remediation actions (if applicable)

Cost management

As Azure environments scale, it remains important to keep consumption under control, traceable and manageable. Azure environments are typically used to host applications from different company departments and cost centers. Cegeka cost management leverages different Azure tools to help customers understand their consumption, keep it under desired limits and enable cost allocation, chargeback or showback strategies.

Services

  • Alert on predefined consumption budgets
  • Review expiring Azure reservations

Cost Optimization

Azure offers multiple ways to consume its resources and the charges also generally depend from the use duration or sizes of selected resources. Consequently, it is important to keep azure consumption aligned with real needs.

The cost optimization service of Cegeka is meant to optimize the consumption by continuously scanning deployed resources, providing optimization recommendations and reduce charges.

Services

  • Identify unused resources
  • Identify oversized resources via Azure Advisor
  • Evaluate consumption models:
    • Pay As You Go vs reservations
  • Evaluate licensing optimisations through Azure Hybrid Benefit
  • Evaluate usage of Resource Snoozing on cost
  • Evaluate usage of Autoscaling on cost

Microsoft Azure Individual Components

Besides the base service Cegeka delivers several other services for Microsoft Azure. Typically these services matches the services delivered by Microsoft. Cegeka performs additional services in this regard.

Azure VMs

Azure virtual machines like traditional VM’s provide full control on inner content (operating system, application & configuration) to administrators. However, while they do not compare with platform features built in other PaaS offerings, Azure VM’s offer more features than traditional VM’s and Cegeka can manage these features making Azure VM’s more cloud native.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Troubleshoot VM related Backup and restore issues
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management
  • Monitor Encryption keys (if applicable)

Addon: Site Recovery

A prerequisite for this add on is that an existing Disaster Recovery Plan is in place.

  • Include VM in DR-Plan
  • Monitor replication of VM
  • Manage agent health
  • Keep agent up to date

Remarks

Some features are covered by others services

  • Network & Network Security Groups: included in the base service
  • Operating system management: separate service

Storage accounts

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Monitor Encryption keys (if applicable)

Azure Bastion

Azure Bastion provides secure and seamless RDP/SSH connectivity to all virtual machines in a VNet, directly in the Azure portal over TLS. With Azure Bastion, virtual machines do not need a public IP address and their RDP/SSH ports is not exposed to the outside world. Azure Bastion provides access to the virtual machine directly from the Azure portal without additional client, agent, or piece of software.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)

AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service)

While containers exist since a long time, they gained more substantial adoption over the last years. During this period of wider adoption, different container orchestration platforms co-existed for a while but Kubernetes reached the highest consensus and is now considered as the standard container orchestration platform, among other thanks to its ability to preserve container portability across on-premises and multi-cloud environments (in contrast to other proprietary solutions).

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Defender for containers

App service

Azure App service allows users to easily deploy, build and scale web apps created with .NET, .NET Core, Node.JS, Java, PHP, Ruby or Python, in containers or running on any operating system.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Yearly certificate renewal
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure cache for Redis

Azure Cache for Redis is a fully managed, in-memory cache that enables high-performance and scalable architectures. Use it to create cloud or hybrid deployments that handle millions of requests per second at sub-millisecond latency – all with the configuration, security and availability benefits of a managed service.

While most databases store data on slower, disk-based storage, Azure Cache for Redis stores data in memory. Since memory is significantly faster than disk storage, data can be written and retrieved much more quickly.

Adding Azure Cache for Redis can increase data throughput by over 800 percent while increasing latency performance by over 1,000 percent at a cost-effective price. It’s a great way to efficiently scale applications and improve user experience without the expense of rearchitecting your underlying database.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Logic App

Like Azure Function, Logic App also helps developers to build modern application architecture. Logic App provides an orchestration platform to build automated workflows. Logic App provides extensive built-in connectors with third-party (Salesforce, SAP, Oracle, Twitter,…) or Microsoft (O365, Dynamics, BizTalk, Azure,…) services for simple enterprise integration services. It is not only serverless but also requires no code at all. Cegeka can help developers to adopt this kind of patterns by offloading non application-level activities, allowing developers to focus on the business and application logic.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Function

Azure Function allows modern application architectures to be created, including patterns like event-based processing, microservices, LowCode (leveraging built-in features & integration), serverless, auto-scaling, cloud native (platform integration)… Cegeka can help developers to adopt this kind of patterns by offloading them from non application-level activities, allowing them to focus on the business and application logic.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Files

Azure files allows organizations to host SMB or NFS file shares in the cloud without managing server infrastructure.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Queues

Azure Queue Storage provides storage for large numbers of messages. Messages can be accessed from anywhere in the world via authenticated calls using HTTP or HTTPS and queues can scale very high (millions of messages). Messaging services are used to decouple applications, providing scalability based on asynchronous processing. Azure Queues is a simple messaging service providing very high scalability and complementing more advanced messaging solutions of Azure like Event Grid & Service Bus.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Tables

Azure Table storage provides structured NoSQL data storage, providing a key/value store with a schema-less design. Because Table storage is schema-less, it's easy to adapt data structure as application needs evolve. While Azure Table provides a simple and cost efficient NoSQL table storage, it could be at term be replaced by Azure Cosmos DB Table API.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure SQL

Azure SQL provides a PaaS platform to host SQL workloads. It provides different form factors with different customization and compliancy levels with on-premises workloads, including SQL managed instances, SQL databases & elastic pools.

The service described below focusses on managing the functionalities provided by Azure SQL allowing to host SQL workloads, it does not include pure database management.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Database for MySQL

Azure Database for MySQL provides a PaaS platform to host MySQL workloads. It provides multiple tiers and consumption model like reservations or PAYG.

The service described below focusses on managing the functionalities provided by Azure to support and integrate Azure Database for MySQL and to keep it safe and secure, it does not include pure data management tasks.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)

Azure Database for PostgreSQL

Azure Database for PostgreSQL provides a PaaS platform to host PostgreSQL workloads. It provides multiple tiers and consumption model like reservations or PAYG.

The service described below focusses on managing the functionalities provided by Azure to support and integrate Azure Database for PostgreSQL and to keep it safe and secure, it does not include pure data management tasks.

Azure Database for MariaDB

Azure Database for MariaDB provides a PaaS platform to host MariaDB workloads. It provides multiple tiers and consumption model like reservations or PAYG.

The service described below focusses on managing the functionalities provided by Azure to support and integrate Azure Database for MariaDB and to keep it safe and secure, it does not include pure data management tasks.

Azure container registry

Azure container registry provides storage and management for container images. It integrates closely with different other Azure services like AKS, App Service and Azure pipelines but can also be used with third party products like Docker Swarm, DC/OS,…

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)
  • Check unused container images

Azure Key Vault

Azure Key Vault provides a central location to securely store keys, passwords, certificates, and other secrets, eliminating unsafe practices like storing credentials inside application code, blob storage or developer files,…

Azure Key Vault features multiple layers of redundancy to make sure that keys and secrets remain available to application even if individual components of the service fail.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Site Recovery

Azure Site Recovery (ASR) provides disaster recovery service, typically between two Azure regions but it can also recover on-premises VMs to Azure or to another on-premises datacenter. ASR restores VMs based on recovery points which can be of two types: crash-consistent and app-consistent recovery points. Crash-consistent recovery points only include disk data while app-consistent ones also include memory data flushed by the application at snapshot time. If desired, ASR can also automatically deploy the target environment (subscription, resource group, vnet, storage account, replica managed disks, availability zones & availability sets). Target VMs are only created when a failover is initiated (avoiding useless charges). By default, the failover occurs manually (single click/cli command) but could be scripted if trigger conditions can be clearly identified.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)
  • Manage recovery plans (test & production plans)
  • Yearly execution of a test recovery plan (without production impact)

Azure DNS

Azure DNS is a hosting service for DNS domains that provides name resolution by using Microsoft Azure infrastructure. By hosting your domains in Azure, you can manage your DNS records by using the same credentials, APIs, tools, and billing as your other Azure services.

Azure DNS consists in a global network of DNS name server allowing Azure to offer a 100% SLA on DNS request resolution. Azure DNS can host private and public domains. In the case of private domains, Azure DNS also provides auto-registration of Azure VMs located in a VNet (auto-registration applies to a single VNet).

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure S2S VPN Connection

An Azure site-to-site VPN connection is a virtual private network (VPN) connection between an on-premises network and an Azure Virtual Network (VNet). This connection allows traffic to flow securely between the on-premises network and the VNet in Azure over the internet.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure ExpressRoute (optional)

Azure ExpressRoute (ER) provides a dedicated connection between Microsoft cloud (including Azure and O365 services) and a telecommunication provider extending the connectivity to a final point, typically a customer location. An ExpressRoute is an alternative to Internet providing dedicated bandwidth and private connectivity (non encrypted by default). It can be combined with a VPN gateway or another ExpressRoute to provide high-availability.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure CDN

Azure CDN provides content delivery acceleration to end users thanks to Azure worldwide network and by leveraging the closest POP to serve user requests. Azure CDN accelerates both static and dynamic content delivery. Static content can be cached and be subsequently served locally from the closest PoP (offloading also central back-end resources). Dynamic content delivery can also be accelerated by leveraging Microsoft backbone from the closest PoP to the backend resource (instead of the Internet backbone) and by leveraging smart routing optimizations.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Load Balancer

An Azure Load Balancer improves application performance and high-availability by distributing client requests between a set of equivalent servers and by continuously checking their health. An Azure Load Balancer helps applications to scale horizontally and remain available in case of local failure or outage impacting an entire availability zone.

Moreover, Load Balancer belongs to the rich suite of Azure load-balancing solutions and is specially meant for L4 load-balancing in a single region, other solutions are available for single region L7 load-balancing (Application gateway) and for multi-region HTTP(S)/DNS-based load-balancing (respectively Front Door and Traffic Manager).

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Application Gateway

An Azure Application Gateway provides the same application performance, scalability and high-availability as a Load Balancer but it also provides additional benefits by its ability to process L7 (HTTP/HTTPS) attributes. An application Gateway is able to make optimal routing decisions based on HTTP(S) URI, headers or query strings. It is also able to terminate TLS sessions, providing data encryption in transit. It is also able to make deeper evaluation of the health of backend resources, including their ability to properly answer HTTP or custom probes. Finally an Application Gateway can optionally be used as a WAF or as an ingress for AKS.

Moreover, Application Gateway belongs to the rich suite of Azure load-balancing solutions and is specially meant for L7 load-balancing in a single region, other solutions are available for single region L4 load-balancing (Load Balancer) and for multi-region HTTP(S)/DNS-based load-balancing (respectively Front Door and Traffic Manager).

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Traffic Manager

Azure Traffic Manager is a DNS-based traffic load balancer. This service allows you to distribute traffic to your public facing applications across the global Azure regions. Traffic Manager also provides your public endpoints with high availability and quick responsiveness.

Traffic Manager uses DNS to direct the client requests to the appropriate service endpoint based on a traffic-routing method. Traffic manager also provides health monitoring for every endpoint. The endpoint can be any Internet-facing service hosted inside or outside of Azure. Traffic Manager provides a range of traffic-routing methods and endpoint monitoring options to suit different application needs and automatic failover models. Traffic Manager is resilient to failure, including the failure of an entire Azure region.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Firewall

Azure Firewall complements the network filtering function provided by Network Security Groups (NSGs) by adding a few additional benefits including: central management, fully stateful firewall, NAT, FQDN filtering, Threat-intelligence filtering (based on malicious IP and domains). Compared to third-party solutions, it also provides native high-availability and unrestricted scalability.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Service Bus

Service Bus provides message brokering services allowing application components to be decoupled and still communicate efficiently and whatever their location, in Azure or on-premises. It supports modern application architecture by enabling asynchronous messaging, transaction processing (with ordered delivery) and consumer load-balancing.

Cegeka can help developers to adopt this kind of patterns by offloading them from non application-level activities, allowing them to focus on the business and application logic.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Analysis service

Built on SQL Server Analysis Service (SSAS), Azure Analysis Service is a serverless and cloud native data modelling service to analyse and combine data from multiple sources.

Azure Databricks

Azure Databricks is a cloud native deployment of Spark based analytics

Azure Data factory

Built from SQL SSIS, Azure Data Factory is a cloud native service to build & run data integration pipelines, including ETL or ELT processing. This service allows raw big data from multiple sources to be cleaned and centralised for further processing or analysis.

  • Monitoring

  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)

Azure Data lake storage

Data Lake Storage builds on blob storage and extends its native features and benefits (cheap, massively scalable and durable storage) to big data analytics use cases thanks to additional features like file system semantics and file-level security.

Azure Machine Learning

Azure machine learning allows a tenant to create a model in Azure Machine Learning or use an open-source model and run them in Azure.

  • Monitoring

  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)

Azure Purview

Azure Purview provides a holistic overview and governance service of a tenant data landscape, whatever the location of the data, including Azure, other clouds, on-premises and SaaS data. Governance services include among other data discovery, classification and lineage in general.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring

  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Azure Synapse Analytics

Formerly known as SQL data warehouse, Azure Synapse Analytics provides a complete big data analysis and integration platform. This includes data warehouse storage (based on Azure Data Lake), SQL & T-SQL distributed query service (including support for streaming data and machine learning models), Spark big data engine, ETL/ELT integration pipelines (similar to Azure data factory pipelines)

  • Incident handling

  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

API management

API management provides a central point to expose APIs securely and at scale. It allows application owner to define API consumption policies like quota & throttling. It also simplifies API consumption by means of a developer portal for consumer onboarding (API documentation, interactive console, account creation, quota visibility,…) & productization. Finally it also provides mechanisms to control their lifecycle, including updates & visibility.

Cegeka can help developers to adopt this kind of patterns by offloading them from non application-level activities, allowing them to focus on the API logic.

  • Incident handling
  • Monitoring
  • Access control
  • Governance (resource hierarchy, tagging & naming convention)
  • Compliance review (when subject to non Cegeka-standard Azure policy rules)
  • Capacity management (tiering, node quantity,…)

Workplace Services

Remote workplace Services (Citrix)

The Remote workplace services is the service that manages the Central workspace infrastructure, a virtual workspace located at Cegeka’s datacenters and remotely available for end-users. This service manages the following topics:

  • Citrix as infrastructure for published applications/desktops;
  • Ivanti Workspace Manager Suite as desktop management solution (security and profile management).

Introduction

To manage the user settings, Cegeka will use workplace management software.

The following components are currently being supported by Cegeka:

  • Management center: centralized management of environment manager, application manager and performance manager roles in the workplace management software;
  • Environment manager: environment manager manages complex and diverse combinations of users, apps, and data across enterprise desktop environments;
  • Application manager: enables application entitlements which can be defined on a per-user basis based on factors such as location, device name, IP address, firewall settings, even time of day;
  • Performance manager: Key computing resources are now under IT's control by this application.

Service description

  • Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Configuration:
    • Managing of global configuration (global settings) for the environment manager, application manager and performance manager server roles;
    • Backing up, restoring and cleaning of application profiles (personalization) for environment manager;
    • Applying of environment manager polices (where <klant> provides environment related policies);
    • Applying of application manager rules (where <klant> provides application related security controls);
    • Managing of application (software) black/white list;
    • Managing of performance manager application settings.
  • Agent deployment:
    • Installation of environment manager, application manager, performance manager, client communication agents for clients/servers under service scope.
  • Security administration:
    • Managing of application security on per-user/ user groups bases per locations, devices, IP ranges.
  • Troubleshooting:
    • Troubleshooting of environment manager, application manager, performance manager related issues.
  • Optional services
  • Updating:
    • Patching and installing hotfixes to all workplace management components;
    • Delivering cumulative updates;
    • Updating test environment.
  • Design:
    • Designing and testing new environment manager policies;
    • Creating of new environment manager policies;
    • Reporting;
    • Provide access to existing reports;
    • Publishing of reports for <klant>;
    • Creating any report based on generic workplace management software reports.
  • • Application Packaging (New or updated applications):
    • See description under chapter 9.4.2. Workplace application service (Optional services)

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

I

R, A

R, A

I

R, A

C, I

R, A

R, A

I

Citrix client configuration

Troubleshoot client connection problems to Citrix XenApp server (server-side)

I

R, A

Communicate Changes to client connection strategy on a timely basis

I

R, A

Cegeka and <klant> will mutually agree scheduling of Citrix ICA client update

I, C

R, A

Define configuration and group policy settings of local Citrix ICA client in line with overall <klant> policies

I

Implement configuration and group policy settings of local Citrix ICA client
