Service Part number(s)

O.0580_0010_0010_M_S.000 Citrix General Services Silver incl. PVS patch management (max. 5)

Application launcher (Liquit)

Introduction

Cegeka provides <klant> end-user a one-stop-shop for accessing applications and IT resources.

Cegeka realizes this through the use of application launcher.

The application launcher allows end-users to seamlessly launch any type of application whether they are locally installed, streamed or published as Citrix applications. Integration with SaaS applications is available off the shelf.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • System management and administration, software configuration control and maintenance, event and fault monitoring, logging, alerting and reporting on solution layer for the central application launcher implementation in the Cegeka datacenter.
  • Operate and maintain the application launcher agents on managed systems.
  • System management and administration, software configuration control and maintenance, event and fault monitoring, logging, alerting and reporting on solution layer for the web portal.
  • Configuration of overall policies and global settings on the back-end.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<KLANT>

CEGEKA

Define user rights policy

R, A

C, I

Define requirements about application launch limits based on user rights policy (e.g. location)

IC

R, A

Implement application launch limits

I

R, A

Implement policies and settings

I

R, A

Service Part number(s)

O.0580_0010_0040_M_S.000 Liquit Application Launcher Management

Enterprise mobility management

Introduction

The smartphone service involves some combination of Mobile Device Management (MDM), Mobile Application Management (MAM) and Mobile Content Management (MCM). MDM focuses on locking down mobile devices, while MAM focuses on controlling which users can access which applications and MCM focuses on allowing only approved applications to access corporate data or transmit it.

This Service provides activities on all above combinations.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

Mobile Device Management (MDM)

  • Manages and controls iOS and Android (including Samsung SAFE) devices;
  • Configures device policies and deploys them over-the-air;
  • Enforces built-in security features such as passcodes and device encryption;
  • Provides full loss and theft protection for lost or stolen devices;
  • Builds group-based compliance policies.

Mobile Application Management (MAM)

  • Securely distributes apps to individual users or groups;
  • Deploys iOS-managed, android and windows apps for added control over app data;
  • Blacklists apps that might be risky or time-wasting;
  • Supports enterprise purchasing of apps via Apple's Volume Purchasing (VPP).

Mobile Content Management (MCM)

  • Accesses content published from Enterprise Content store or from various cloud storage providers like Dropbox, Google Drive, Microsoft OneDrive, and various WebDAV compatible solutions;
  • Adding or editing documents, annotating on PDFs and filling forms support collaboration across the enterprise, regardless if you are in the office or using a mobile device while on the road;
  • Transparently encrypts each file, letting you rest assured that your documents and data remain safe—not just in the office, but everywhere your users go.

Optional services

This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0560_0020_0010_A_TM.000 Mobile device management (Intune)

O.0560_0030_0010_A_TM.000 Mobile application management (Intune)

User virtualization

Introduction

To manage the user settings, Cegeka will use workplace management software.

The following components are currently being supported by Cegeka:

  • Management center: centralized management of environment manager, application manager and performance manager roles in the workplace management software;
  • Environment manager: environment manager manages complex and diverse combinations of users, apps, and data across enterprise desktop environments;
  • Application manager: enables application entitlements which can be defined on a per-user basis based on factors such as location, device name, IP address, firewall settings, even time of day;
  • Performance manager: Key computing resources are now under IT's control by this application.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Configuration:
    • Managing of global configuration (global settings) for the environment manager, application manager and performance manager server roles;
    • Backing up, restoring and cleaning of application profiles (personalization) for environment manager;
    • Applying of environment manager polices (where customer provides environment related policies);
    • Applying of application manager rules (where customer provides application related security controls);
    • Managing of application (software) black/white list;
    • Managing of performance manager application settings.
  • Agent deployment:
    • Installation of environment manager, application manager, performance manager, client communication agents for clients/servers under service scope.
  • Security administration:
    • Managing of application security on per-user/ user groups bases per locations, devices, IP ranges.
  • Troubleshooting:
    • Troubleshooting of environment manager, application manager, performance manager related issues.

Optional services

  • Updating:
    • Patching and installing hotfixes to all workplace management components;
    • Delivering cumulative updates;
    • Updating test environment.
  • Design:
    • Designing and testing new environment manager policies;
    • Creating of new environment manager policies;
    • Reporting;
    • Provide access to existing reports;
    • Publishing of reports for customer;
    • Creating any report based on generic workplace management software reports.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Citrix management

Introduction

As part of the virtual workstation service Cegeka provides a virtual workstation which functions as a ‘desktop as a Service’. Furthermore, individual applications will be made available to managed devices through application virtualization.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Management of the Hypervisor layer used to host the Citrix XenApp Servers;
  • Management of the Citrix XenApp components;
  • Management of the provisioning server;
  • Minor platform updates;
  • Performance tuning and capacity planning of the Citrix Platform;
  • Capacity monitoring and management of the Citrix Platform, adjustments within the platform hardware boundaries;
  • Routine audits of the Citrix platform components;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and proactive resolving of potential issues;
  • Documentation of system configurations;

Optional services

  • External access to the Citrix Service;
  • Updates to the Citrix provisioning Image via the change management process;
  • Provisioning of new applications via the change management process;

Platform upgrades via the change management process.

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0580_0010_0010_M_S.000 Citrix General Services Silver incl. PVS patchmanagement (max. 5)

Microsoft 365

Introduction

The Office 365 service includes the following components:

  • Management of:
    • Active Directory Federation Services management
    • AD Sync (DirSync)
    • Yammer DirSync
    • Office 365 Management portal that includes
      • Exchange Online
      • SharePoint Online
      • Teams
      • OneDrive
      • Yammer
  • Monitoring
  • Reporting and CSI
  • Antivirus (includes antispam)
  • Backup in scope for the on premise components
  • Management of the Backup solution for cloud data

For the portal management, we have the following items included:

  • Get Service Health, Service Requests, Mail protection, Message Center;
  • View, bills, license, notifications;
  • Document the issues resolution and maintain knowledge base;

Service description

Standard Services

Troubleshoot OneDrive for Business issues

Manage user quotas

Manage access delegation

Manage access control

Manage data encryption

Manage external sharing

Manage auditing

Manage notifications

Manage syncing of file types

Manage retention policies

Use GPOs to control OneDrive for Business clients

Troubleshoot Identity issues

Troubleshoot AD Connect synchronization issues

Create and maintain documentation

Manage users in Microsoft 365 admin center

Manage Azure AD Connect

Manage ADFS

Manage Relying Party Trusts

Manage Certificates

Manage cloud identities

Manage MFA for Microsoft 365

Advanced Threat Protection

Conditional access

Exchange Online Protection

Encryption

Auditing

Troubleshoot email issues

Create and maintain documentation

Manage email policies

Manage mailbox delegation

Manage mailbox quotas

Manage retention policies,  auditing, retention tags and journal rules

Manage mobile device access policies

Manage inactive mailboxes

Manage public folders

Manage Advanced Threat Protection

Manage mail encryption

Manage anti-spam

Manage quarantine

Manage mailflow

Manage disclaimers

Manage unified messaging

Manage hybrid configuration

Manage SPF records in public DNS2

Manage Online archives

Manage journaling

Troubleshoot SharePoint Online issues

Troubleshoot AAM (Alternate access mappings) configuration issues

Create and maintain documentation

Manage SharePoint permissions

Manage the recycle bin

Manage SharePoint storage

Manage site collections and global settings in the SharePoint admin center

Manage administrators for a site collection

Manage external sharing for SharePoint Online

Manage site collection storage limits

Manage synchronization with OneDrive for Business

Manage limits and quotas

Manage retention policies

Manage hybrid picker

Optional services

(time and material or project-based activities)

  • Functional End user support
  • Management of Active Directory outside the scope of ADFS and DirSync
  • Hardware and software procurement
  • Local network support
  • Training of users
  • Applications migration
  • Personalize SharePoint sites according to user preferences
  • Customize Login pages
  • Analyse and propose SharePoint solutions when user has any new requirement to be implemented
  • Implement any changes in methodology that SharePoint will bring
  • Issues related to the MS Outlook client configuration
  • Resolving issues with non-Out of the box product installations/features

Out of scope services

The currently defined service does not cover the following components:

  • Office applications
  • Project Portfolio Management
  • Business Intelligence Software
  • Information Rights management
  • 0365 licenses

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0590_0010_0040_M_TM.000 Per mailbox - Office 365 mgmnt (ADFS, ADFS Proxy etc.)

O.0590_0010_0060_M_TM.000 PerS4B user - Office 365 mgmnt

Office 365 support Service

Introduction

With this Service users are able to have access to the following:

  • a mailbox for sending and receiving email and the use of the agenda functionality;
  • a personal drive for storing user data;
  • access to applications like SharePoint, Teams, Power BI.

Service description

Scope standard services

In scope of this service are activities performed using the Office 365 Admin Center as well as technical support. The service includes the following components:

  • Office 365 Identity and Access
  • Exchange Online
  • Skype for Business Online
  • SharePoint Online
  • OneDrive for Business
  • Yammer
  • Teams
  • General Services

Standard Services

  • Cegeka shall perform, but not limited to, the following activities as part of this Service:

Office 365 Identity and Access

In scope of this component are the following:

  • Cloud Identity management
  • Synchronized Identity management
  • Federated Identity and Single Sign On

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot Identity issues
  • Troubleshoot AD Connect synchronization issues
  • Create and maintain documentation
  • Manage users in Office 365 admin center
  • Manage MFA for Office 365
  • Manage Azure AD Connect
  • Manage ADFS
  • Manage Relying Party Trusts
  • Manage Certificates

Exchange Online

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot email issues
  • Create and maintain documentation
  • Manage email policies
  • Manage Online archives
  • Manage journaling
  • Manage mailbox delegation
  • Manage mailbox quotas
  • Manage retention policies, auditing, retention tags and journal rules
  • Manage mobile device access policies
  • Manage inactive mailboxes
  • Manage public folders
  • Manage Advanced Threat Protection
  • Manage mail encryption
  • Manage anti-spam[3]
  • Manage quarantine
  • Manage mail flow
  • Manage disclaimers
  • Manage unified messaging
  • Manage hybrid configuration
  • Manage SPF records in public DNS[4]

Skype for Business Online

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot Skype for Business Online issues
  • Create and maintain documentation
  • Manage external communication settings
  • Manage audio conferencing
  • Manage online meetings
  • Manage Office 365 URLs and IP address ranges
  • Manage federation between on premise Skype for Business deployment and Online deployment (requires TAM Skype for Business for the on-premises Skype environment)
  • Manage autodis cover
  • Manage logging
  • Manage SRV and CNAME records in public DNS[5]

SharePoint Online[6]

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot SharePoint Online issues
  • Troubleshoot AAM (Alternate access mappings) configuration issues
  • Create and maintain documentation
  • Manage SharePoint permissions
  • Manage the recycle bin
  • Manage SharePoint storage
  • Manage site collections and global settings in the SharePoint admin center
  • Manage administrators for a site collection
  • Manage external sharing for SharePoint Online
  • Manage site collection storage limits
  • Manage synchronization with OneDrive for Business
  • Manage limits and quotas
  • Manage retention policies
  • Manage hybrid picker

OneDrive for Business

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot OneDrive for Business issues
  • Manage user quotas
  • Manage access delegation
  • Manage access control
  • Manage data encryption
  • Manage external sharing
  • Manage auditing
  • Manage notifications
  • Manage syncing of file types
  • Manage retention policies
  • Use GPOs to control OneDrive for Business client[7]

Yammer

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot Yammer issues
  • Manage Yammer internal Home network
  • Manage Yammer external network
  • Manage Yammer groups
  • Manage user lifecycle
  • Manage domain lifecycle
  • Manage content and security
  • Manage IP-range restrictions
  • Manage password policies
  • Manage data retention policies
  • Manage Directory integration[8]

Teams

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Troubleshoot Teams client issues
  • Manage Teams Portal settings
  • Manage the auto-created Exchange Online group mailbox
  • Manage audio conferencing
  • Manage connectors
  • Manage conditional access policies

General Services

The included activities can be found in the list below:

  • Monitoring is in scope for the on-premise components only (e.g.: ADFS, WAP, Certificates).
  • Security components:
  • Manage Multi Factor Authentication for Office 365
  • Manage inactive mailboxes in Exchange Online
  • Manage self-service password reset
  • Manage group-based licensing
  • Manage Single Sign-On
  • Manage risk-based conditional access
  • Manage device access policies
  • Manage Data Loss Prevention across Office 365 services
  • Manage Exchange Online advanced threat protection
  • Manage mail and data encryption
  • Manage auditing

Not in scope

  • Functional End user support
  • Management of Active Directory outside the scope of ADFS and AD Connect (e.g.: Replication)
  • The installation, configuration and lifecycle management of client applications (e.g. Outlook, Teams client) is handled through the Software and OS deployment Services.
  • The currently defined service does not cover other components than the ones listed as in scope. Any other component that the vendor (Microsoft) will include in the future will be analysed during periodic service reviews.
  • Third-party backup tools or solutions for Office 365 Online components.

Conditions

  • A Cegeka Service Desk service must be present in order to deliver this service.
  • OS management is required for the on-premise server roles (servers holding the ADFS, AD Connect and database roles).
  • AD Management is required for the ADFS and AD Connect servers.

Service Part number(s)

O.0590_0010_0040_M_TM.000 Per mailbox - Office 365 mgmnt (ADFS, ADFS Proxy etc.)

O.0590_0010_0060_M_TM.000 PerS4B user - Office 365 mgmnt

Directory Services (User Service)

Introduction

Cegeka’s user Service is based on Microsoft Active Directory Technology. This Service gives the ability for end-users to logon and logoff to the corporate network or to a federated (cloud) Service. It identifies all resources on a network and makes them accessible to users and applications. Resources include e-mail addresses, computers and peripheral devices such as printers. The directory Service makes the physical network topology and protocols transparent so that a user on a network can access any resource without knowing where or how it is physically connected. This Service provides basic authentication for users & resources.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Deployment of the different AD server components corresponding to customer’s non-functional requirements and agreed SLA’s;
  • DHCP management;
  • DNS management (non-public IP’s);
  • Management of security components for AD;
  • Capacity management of the directory service infrastructure;
  • Performance tuning of the directory service infrastructure;
  • AD software and patch management;
  • Routine audits of the directory services components;
  • Analysis of system logs, identification and proactive resolving of potential issues;
  • Directory Service infrastructure documentation;
  • Technical user management is in scope;
  • Technical Management of Active Directory Connect for SSO to web-enabled (& SaaS) applications;
  • Backup and restore.

Optional services

  • Federated identity management

Out of scope

User management

Conditions

  • The Cegeka managed services personnel needs to have a login with Enterprise Admin access on the entire Directory Services Infrastructure.
  • The Servers on which the Directory Services are implemented must be under the management of Cegeka.
  • The design and installation of the Active Directory Infrastructure must have been the responsibility of Cegeka. If this condition is not met, then the standard set of features may not be fully available.

Part number

O.0610_0010_0010_M_TM.000 Directory Service - Active Directory Service (incl. DNS & DHCP)

File Services

Introduction

File Services are required for personal or group file storage. The file service provides users access to the central storage medium.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Manage file shares;
  • Manage file system security;
  • Manage DFS (name spaces, referrals);
  • Manage DFS replication;
  • Backup and restore of files;
  • Anti-virus service (including updates);

Optional services

This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

This Service does not include the File Server environment itself.

Conditions

  • The availability of a system functioning as File Server.
  • A functional Active Directory.

Part number

O.0600_0010_0020_M_TM.000 File Services - Per user - 501-1000 users

Local Workplace Services (Image & OS Build Maintenance)

Introduction

The software distribution service delivers all activities required for packaging and distribution of all workplace applications and operating systems (Windows 10). What applications are packaged is agreed upon between <klant> and Cegeka. As part of this element of Service, Cegeka keeps track of what applications are part of the standard package for <klant>. Cegeka shall take care that at least the most recent security and vulnerability patches are installed for all software included in the image. By means of the software distribution service, Cegeka also facilitates the rollout of new releases, patches and service packs for the standard workplace image.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible for providing the following Services:

  • Detect & delete viruses on deployment servers (SCEP);
  • Monitor, manage & report capacity on deployment & management servers;
  • Install & configure discussed upgrades on deployment infrastructure;
  • Backup main deployment & management server;
  • Operate & maintain the software distribution workstation agents on managed systems;
  • Management of the software distribution workstation agents (install, remove, update) on managed systems;
  • Provide up-to-date list of authorized users, groups, profiles for each software package and application assignments;
  • Ensure software distribution for mandatory application assignments for authorized users, groups or business profiles;
  • Ensure software distribution for optional application assignments for authorized users, groups or business profiles;
  • Synchronization of software and images towards different distribution points;
  • OS management of the distribution point;

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<KLANT>

CEGEKA

Create and maintain OS image

R, A

Package applications

R, A

Distribute applications

R, A

Workplace hardening

C

R, A

Service Part number(s)

O.0560_0010_0010_A_TM.000 Image & OS Build Maintenance service - Fat client management

Remote access Service (Always-on-VPN)

Introduction

The remote access service is a service that provides remote access to the <klant> network for all <klant> end-user.

This remote access functionality offers the <klant> employees the possibility to access (remotely) via the internet platform the <klant> network. The access is realized via Always-on-VPN.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services:

  • Monitoring, resolving issues & reporting on the Always-on-VPN solution.

Optional services

  • This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • N/A

Conditions

  • This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Responsibilities

ACTIVITY MATRIX

<KLANT>

CEGEKA

Manage or always-on-vpn servers

I

R, A

Provide access to users through directory services

I

R, A

Service Part number(s)

O.0560_0010_0010_A_TM.000 Image & OS Build Maintenance service - Fat client management (Included in)

Workplace application Service

Introduction

Through the application service all customer approved workplace applications are managed and maintained. Cegeka is responsible for patching workplace applications and takes care that applications are up-to-date. Customer approval is required prior to the rollout of a new release, patch or service pack.

Service description

Standard Services

Cegeka shall be responsible for providing the following Services:

  • All applications will be installed through Cegeka’s service;
  • These applications are either standard client apps or business apps;
  • Users can request new software. Once approved, Cegeka supports in making the software available to the user through the application launcher;
  • Customer will indicate what workplace applications should be made available by means of an automated distribution and what workplace applications are part of the basic workplace image;
  • Cegeka carries out management of these workplace applications.

Optional services

This service does not contain any optional services.

Out of scope

  • Support for unauthorized applications installed by the customer end-user themselves is not part of the Services delivered by Cegeka.
  • Re-packaging applications.
  • Automatic updates are included, but no custom re-packaging

Conditions

This Service does not require any explicit conditions to be met.

Part number

O.0620_0020_0010_M_TM.000 Application Deployment Service (Per SCCM platform)

Mail and Calendaring

Introduction

Microsoft Exchange is our proposed solution for the messaging services. The messaging platform is integrated with the Active Directory, which provides a central administration model for the users.

All Exchange users will have their Outlook client setup to operate in ‘cached mode’. When users first log on to their mailbox, a copy of their mailbox is downloaded to the client creating an offline store (‘ost’) file. If you work offline, whether by choice or due to a connection problem, your data is still available to you wherever you are.

The Calendar function is a standard feature of Exchange. In collaboration with Outlook, users are able to schedule their free / busy time and share that information with other Exchange users. Resource mailboxes can be assigned for conference room booking with Exchange. A user locates the conference room name from the Global Address List within his / her Outlook client and schedules a meeting time against that room. These resource mailboxes are shared for all users giving any user the ability to see room availability.

Service description

Standard features

The service mail & calendaring consists of:

  • Installation, configuration and management of all Microsoft Exchange
  • Maintain documentation of the environment
  • Apply and maintain messaging & collaboration policies as specified by the customer (on mailbox sizes, archiving rules etc.)
  • Create, maintain and delete User accounts and mailboxes
  • Create regional administrative and service messaging accounts and assign roles to these accounts
  • Perform mailbox and mail item restores as requested by Users
  • Move mailboxes between regions, sectors, divisions, and groups
  • Design, or assist in designing, and implement an anti-spam strategy and policy
  • Create, maintain and assign rights to the Distribution Lists and Global Distribution List management

Optional features

  • Scan e-mail for viruses and quarantine affected message

Out of scope

N/A

Conditions

N/A

Microsoft

Print Services

Introduction

The print service delivers the management and operation for the printer queues of all supported and contracted network printers, multi-functionals and plotters. The objective of this Service is to manage the local printing infrastructure from a technical perspective to support printing functionality for authorized users.

This Service includes the operational monitoring and administration for the print management service infrastructure.

Service description

Standard services

Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following services:

  • Print driver management: With this Service, Cegeka will deliver and install print drivers, for all different workplace versions and operating systems in scope;
  • Print queue management: Cegeka will manage the different print queues, operating systems (of the central print management service infrastructure), software and other assets in order to provide seamless printing experience.

Optional services

  • Secure printing: PIN support & follow-me printing.

Out of scope

The provisioning of printers, multifunctional devices and associated supplies are not included in this Service.

Conditions

  • The availability of a system functioning as Print Server.
  • A functional Active Directory

  1. Data management policies are provided by the customer ↑

  2. On request we can deliver this information ↑

  3. Exchange Online Protection only ↑

  4. requires permissions to the external DNS portal ↑

  5. requires permissions to the external DNS portal ↑

  6. Management of SharePoint does not include content management or custom components ↑

  7. requires Active Directory management ↑

  8. requires Active Directory management ↑
